contributions.quote: we introduce an AI co-scientist, a multi-agent system built on Gemini 2.0 … The system's design incorporates a generate, debate, and evolve approach to hypothesis generation, inspired by the scientific method and accelerated by scaling test-time compute.

contributions.statement: A multi-agent architecture with an asynchronous task execution framework enables flexible compute scaling for hypothesis-generation pipelines in scientific discovery systems.
contributions.quote: Key contributions include: (1) a multi-agent architecture with an asynchronous task execution framework for flexible compute scaling

contributions.statement: A tournament evolution process — where hypotheses compete, win/loss patterns are identified, and losing hypotheses are iteratively revised — enables self-improving hypothesis generation in AI-assisted scientific discovery.
contributions.quote: (2) a tournament evolution process for self-improving hypotheses generation … tournaments that compare and rank hypotheses via the process of finding win and loss patterns, and a hypothesis evolution process to improve their quality.

contributions.statement: AI-generated drug repurposing candidates for acute myeloid leukemia demonstrate tumor inhibition in vitro at clinically applicable concentrations across multiple AML cell lines, validating in silico hypothesis generation for drug repurposing.
contributions.quote: the system proposes candidates with promising validation findings, including candidates for acute myeloid leukemia that show tumor inhibition in vitro at clinically applicable concentrations.

contributions.statement: AI-proposed novel epigenetic targets for liver fibrosis, validated by anti-fibrotic activity and liver cell regeneration in human hepatic organoids, demonstrate that multi-agent hypothesis generation can identify experimentally confirmed therapeutic targets.
contributions.quote: the AI co-scientist proposed new epigenetic targets for liver fibrosis, validated by anti-fibrotic activity and liver cell regeneration in human hepatic organoids.

contributions.statement: BioMARS — a hierarchical multi-agent robotic platform that integrates large language models, vision-language models, and modular dual-arm robotics to autonomously design, plan, and execute biological experiments end-to-end.
contributions.quote: Here we introduce BioMARS (Biological Multi-Agent Robotic System), an intelligent platform that integrates LLMs, VLMs, and modular robotics to autonomously design, plan, and execute biological experiments.

contributions.statement: A tri-agent architecture for laboratory automation comprising a Biologist Agent that synthesizes protocols via retrieval-augmented generation, a Technician Agent that translates protocols into executable robotic pseudo-code, and an Inspector Agent that enforces procedural integrity through multimodal perception and anomaly detection.
contributions.quote: BioMARS uses a hierarchical architecture: the Biologist Agent synthesizes protocols via retrieval-augmented generation; the Technician Agent translates them into executable robotic pseudo-code; and the Inspector Agent ensures procedural integrity through multimodal perception and anomaly detection.

contributions.statement: A VLM- and ViT-powered hierarchical error-detection mechanism for robotic biological execution that identifies geometric misalignments and mechanical failures in real time, triggering replanning or user notification.
contributions.quote: the Inspector Agent—powered by ViTs and VLMs—performs rapid anomaly detection. It identifies procedural deviations including geometric misalignments (e.g., unattached pipette tips, misaligned petri dishes) and mechanical failures, prompting replanning or user notification.

contributions.statement: Fully autonomous cell passaging and culture by a multi-agent robotic system achieves viability, consistency, and morphological integrity matching or exceeding manual human performance.
contributions.quote: The system autonomously conducts cell passaging and culture tasks, matching or exceeding manual performance in viability, consistency, and morphological integrity.

contributions.statement: Context-aware, LLM-driven optimization of biological differentiation protocols — demonstrated on retinal pigment epithelial cells — outperforms conventional protocol strategies by leveraging historical experimental data.
contributions.quote: It also supports context-aware optimization, outperforming conventional strategies in differentiating retinal pigment epithelial cells.

contributions.statement: CRISPR-GPT — a multi-agent LLM system combining chain-of-thought reasoning, retrieval-augmented generation, instruction fine-tuning, and integrated bioinformatics tools — automates end-to-end CRISPR-based gene-editing experiment design and data analysis across four editing modalities (knockout, base editing, prime editing, CRISPRa/i) with tunable automation levels (Meta, Auto, and Q&A modes).
contributions.quote: We introduce CRISPR-GPT, a solution that combines the strengths of LLMs with domain-specific knowledge, chain-of-thought reasoning, instruction fine-tuning, retrieval techniques and tools. CRISPR-GPT is centred around LLM-powered planning and execution agents.

contributions.statement: Gene-editing bench — a benchmark of 288 test cases covering experimental planning, sgRNA design, delivery method selection, CRISPR troubleshooting, and ethics/safety — enables systematic evaluation of AI agents on gene-editing research tasks.
contributions.quote: To assess the AI agent's capabilities to perform gene-editing research, we compiled an evaluation test set, Gene-editing bench, from both public sources and human experts … This test set covers a variety of gene-editing tasks.

contributions.statement: An LLM fine-tuned on open-forum scientific discussions improves domain-specific performance on CRISPR-related biological design problems.
contributions.quote: This system incorporates domain expertise, retrieval techniques, external tools and a specialized LLM fine tuned with open-forum discussions among scientists.

contributions.statement: Fully AI-guided gene-editing workflows executed by junior researchers without prior gene-editing experience successfully knocked out four genes with CRISPR-Cas12a and epigenetically activated two genes with CRISPR-dCas9 on the first attempt, confirmed by editing efficiencies, phenotypes, and protein-level validation.
contributions.quote: We showcase the potential of CRISPR-GPT by knocking out four genes with CRISPR-Cas12a in a human lung adenocarcinoma cell line and epigenetically activating two genes using CRISPR-dCas9 in a human melanoma cell line … All these wet-lab experiments were carried out by junior researchers not familiar with gene editing. They both succeeded on the first attempt.

contributions.statement: AILA (Artificially Intelligent Lab Assistant) — a modular, multi-agent LLM-powered framework with specialized AFM Handler and Data Handler agents — enables end-to-end automation of atomic force microscopy, from experimental design and instrument control to image analysis, via natural-language queries.
contributions.quote: we here introduce AILA (Artificially Intelligent Lab Assistant), an LLM-powered framework augmented with specialized tools

contributions.statement: AFMBench — a benchmark of 100 expertly curated physical AFM tasks spanning experimental workflow design, multi-tool coordination, decision-making, open-ended execution, and data analysis — enables rigorous evaluation of LLM agents against real-world laboratory constraints and hardware variability.
contributions.quote: we develop AFMBench— a comprehensive evaluation suite challenging LLM agents across the complete scientific workflow from experimental design to results analysis

contributions.statement: Strong performance on materials-science question-answering benchmarks does not predict competence in real laboratory automation: LLMs that excel at domain knowledge tasks perform poorly when controlling physical instruments.
contributions.quote: models excelling at materials science question-answering perform poorly in laboratory settings, showing that domain knowledge does not translate to experimental capabilities

contributions.statement: LLM agents deployed in laboratory settings can spontaneously deviate from explicit instructions — a failure mode termed 'sleepwalking' — posing safety-alignment risks for self-driving laboratory applications.
contributions.quote: we observe that LLM agents can deviate from instructions, a phenomenon referred to as sleepwalking, raising safety alignment concerns for SDL applications

contributions.statement: Multi-agent architectures significantly outperform single-agent approaches in laboratory automation tasks, yet both paradigms remain brittle to minor variations in instruction formatting and prompting style.
contributions.quote: Our ablations reveal that multi-agent frameworks significantly outperform single-agent approaches, though both remain sensitive to minor changes in instruction formatting or prompting

contributions.statement: MATDESIGN — a benchmark dataset of real-world materials-discovery goals, constraints, and ground-truth materials/methods extracted from 2024 journal publications — enables evaluation of LLM hypothesis generation beyond model pretraining cutoffs and under realistic application-specific constraints.
contributions.quote: Create a novel benchmark, MATDESIGN, consisting of goals and constraints, along with their corresponding materials and methods.

contributions.statement: ACCELMAT — a tool-free, multi-LLM agentic framework combining a Hypothesis Generation Agent, a multi-model Critic system with iterative feedback, a Summarizer Agent, and an Evaluation Agent — generates and refines materials discovery and design hypotheses across diverse materials and properties without reliance on domain-specific simulation tools.
contributions.quote: Develop an LLM-based agentic framework to generate and refine material discovery and design hypotheses.

contributions.statement: A scalable, LLM-based evaluation metric that scores materials-discovery hypotheses on Closeness to ground truth and Quality dimensions (Alignment, Scientific Plausibility, Novelty, Feasibility, Scalability, Testability, and Impact Potential) mirrors the systematic validation process of domain experts.
contributions.quote: Propose a scalable evaluation metric to measure the relevance and quality of the generated hypotheses for material discovery and design.

contributions.statement: FieldSHIFT — an in-context learning framework that uses a large language model to translate neuroscience paper abstracts into developmental biology abstracts by substituting domain-specific concepts, enabling hypothesis generation at scale across the two fields.
contributions.quote: FieldSHIFT is an in-context learning framework which uses a large language model to convert existing paper abstracts in neuroscience to the field of developmental biology, by appropriately replacing specific words and concepts.

contributions.statement: A paired dataset of neuroscience and developmental bioelectricity sentences and associated concepts for cross-domain abstract translation and hypothesis generation evaluation.
contributions.quote: We release a new dataset for translating between the neuroscience and developmental bioelectricity domains

contributions.statement: Molecular mechanisms underlying cognitive behavior and developmental morphogenesis show a quantitatively significant gene-overlap conservation, validated bioinformatically from FieldSHIFT-generated predictions.
contributions.quote: we test some of the resulting predictions using a bioinformatics approach, revealing a remarkable quantitative conservation of genes between developmental morphogenesis and cognitive behavior … finding a quantitatively significant enrichment in overlap between specific genes implicated in both cognitive processes and morphogenesis.

contributions.statement: Acceleron — a multi-agent LLM-powered interactive co-pilot with distinct mentor and colleague personas — guides researchers through the full ideation pipeline: validating proposal motivation against literature, identifying research gaps, synthesizing plausible solution methods, and designing experimentation strategies.
contributions.quote: We address this gap by introducing 'Acceleron', a 'Co-Pilot' for researchers, designed specifically to accelerate the ideation phase of the research life-cycle. Leveraging the reasoning and domain-specific skills of Large Language Models (LLMs) within an agent-based architecture with distinct personas, Acceleron aids researchers through the formulation of a comprehensive research proposals.

contributions.statement: A two-stage aspect-based retrieval pipeline balances precision and recall in scientific literature retrieval for proposal novelty validation, while also handling unanswerability when no retrieved articles sufficiently address a proposal's motivation.
contributions.quote: it addresses challenges inherent in LLMs, such as hallucinations, implements a two-stage aspect-based retrieval to manage precision-recall trade-offs, and tackles issues of unanswerability

contributions.statement: Mimicking the research ideation process through human-machine interaction — where LLM agents iteratively extract problems, generate analogous sub-problems, and synthesize approaches from existing solutions in the literature — yields qualitatively novel research ideas more effectively than fully automated ideation.
contributions.quote: we are the first ones to mimic the research ideation process using LLMs and execute it using human-machine interaction ensuring accelerated as well as qualitative outcomes, in terms of novel ideas

contributions.statement: KG-CoI (Knowledge Grounded Chain of Ideas) — a system that integrates structured knowledge from knowledge graphs into LLM hypothesis generation via KG-guided context retrieval, KG-augmented chain-of-idea generation, and KG-supported hallucination detection — improves the accuracy and factual grounding of scientifically generated hypotheses.
contributions.quote: We present KG-CoI, a novel LLM-enhanced hypothesis generation system that augments the generated hypotheses with external structured knowledge and presents the result as a coherent chain of ideas.

contributions.statement: A benchmark dataset for evaluating LLM scientific hypothesis generation, constructed by masking links within a knowledge graph and prompting LLMs to hypothesize the withheld relations, enabling quantitative comparison across open- and closed-source models.
contributions.quote: We construct a new dataset to evaluate LLM hypothesis generation and conduct extensive experiments on both open- and close-source LLMs, showing the effectiveness of KG-CoI in hypothesizing scientific knowledge.

contributions.statement: A KG-supported hallucination detection module that verifies individual reasoning steps in a chain-of-ideas against structured knowledge graph facts, reducing hallucinations in LLM-generated scientific reasoning chains.
contributions.quote: We propose a KG-supported hallucination detection method within KG-CoI, which demonstrates the advantage of KG-CoI in reducing hallucinations.

contributions.statement: A formal mathematical decomposition of chemistry hypothesis discovery into three executable subtasks — retrieving inspirations, composing hypotheses from inspirations, and ranking hypotheses — provides a tractable reformulation of an otherwise intractable scientific discovery problem.
contributions.quote: We provide the first mathematical derivation on how to decompose the seemingly impossible-to-solve question into many executable and practical smaller steps. This decomposition make possible to be practical.

contributions.statement: MOOSE-Chem — an agentic LLM framework combining evolutionary algorithm-based background–inspiration association, multi-step inspiration retrieval and composition, and an efficient hypothesis ranking method — enables automated chemistry and materials science hypothesis generation and rediscovery.
contributions.quote: We develop a scientific discovery framework directly based on the mathematical derivation. Different from previous works, we propose an evolutionary algorithm-based method to better associate background and inspiration, multi-step inspiration retrieval and composition, and an efficient ranking method. In addition, the framework can be applied to chemistry and material science, which are not covered by previous methods.

contributions.statement: A benchmark of 51 high-impact chemistry papers (Nature/Science level, published after January 2024), each manually decomposed by PhD chemists into research background, inspirations, and hypothesis, enables contamination-free evaluation of LLM-based scientific hypothesis discovery.
contributions.quote: We construct a benchmark by three chemistry PhD students, consisting of 51 chemistry papers published on Nature, Science, or a similar level, decomposing each paper into the research background, inspirations, and hypothesis.

contributions.statement: LLMs can largely rediscover the core innovations of chemistry hypotheses published in Nature and Science, without data contamination, when given only a research background and a literature corpus.
contributions.quote: For the first time, we show that an LLM-based framework can largely rediscover the main innovations of many chemistry hypotheses that have been published in Nature and Science. The rediscovery is not because of data contamination, because we have controlled the date of the training corpus of the LLM and the online date of the chemistry papers.

contributions.statement: LLMs appear to encode latent scientific knowledge associations — pairs of concepts whose connection enables novel hypothesis generation — that have not yet been explicitly recognized by human scientists, as evidenced by surprisingly high out-of-distribution inspiration retrieval accuracy.
contributions.quote: We propose an assumption, grounded in preliminary experiments, that LLMs may already possess numerous knowledge pairs capable of being associated to create novel knowledge—even when scientists have not previously recognized any relationship between them.

contributions.statement: HypoGeniC — an iterative hypothesis generation algorithm for LLMs inspired by the upper confidence bound (UCB) multi-armed bandit framework — generates and refines data-grounded hypotheses by maintaining a wrong-example bank and a reward-driven exploitation-exploration tradeoff, enabling LLMs to handle arbitrarily large training sets without long-context limitations.
contributions.quote: We propose a novel computational framework for generating and evaluating hypotheses with LLMs.

contributions.statement: Interpretable hypothesis-based classifiers derived from LLM-generated hypotheses outperform few-shot in-context learning by up to 31.7% and exceed supervised learning baselines (RoBERTa, Llama-2-7B) on two challenging real-world datasets, with hypotheses that transfer robustly across different LLMs and out-of-distribution test sets.
contributions.quote: Our generated hypotheses enable interpretable hypothesis-based classifiers that outperform in-context learning and even supervised learning for one synthetic and three real-world datasets. These hypotheses are also robust across different LLMs and out-of-distribution datasets.

contributions.statement: LLM-generated hypotheses about real-world classification tasks both corroborate human-verified theories from existing literature and surface novel, previously undocumented insights — including findings that contradict established feature-engineering results.
contributions.quote: Our generated hypotheses corroborate existing findings while also providing new insights for the tasks.

contributions.statement: HypoBench — a benchmark spanning 194 datasets across 7 real-world and 5 synthetic tasks — enables systematic evaluation of hypothesis generation methods along three dimensions: practical utility (explanatory power), generalizability (in-domain vs. out-of-domain splits), and ground-truth hypothesis discovery rate.
contributions.quote: we introduce HypoBench, a novel benchmark designed to evaluate LLMs and hypothesis generation methods across multiple aspects, including practical utility, generalizability, and hypothesis discovery rate. HypoBench includes 7 real-world tasks and 5 synthetic tasks with 194 distinct datasets.

contributions.statement: A principled conceptual framework distinguishes hypothesis generation (inductive, data-driven explanation of observed phenomena) from research ideation, and operationalizes evaluation criteria centered on explanatory power as the first-order metric, with interestingness as a secondary consideration.
contributions.quote: We develop a systematic and principled framework for benchmarking hypothesis generation and construct the first benchmark accordingly.

contributions.statement: The first head-to-head comparison of six hypothesis-generation methods across four state-of-the-art LLMs shows that augmenting data-driven generation with retrieved literature achieves the best performance on real-world tasks, and that Qwen produces the most effective and generalizable hypotheses among tested models.
contributions.quote: We conduct the first comparison between methods and models for hypothesis generation. In real-world datasets, we find that Literature + Data is the best approach and Qwen is the best model within our choices of models.

contributions.statement: Controlled synthetic datasets with programmatically varied difficulty (via logistic regression or decision-tree data-generating processes) reveal that hypothesis discovery rates collapse sharply as task complexity grows — best-performing model–method combinations recover only 38.8% of ground-truth hypotheses at high difficulty, quantifying a concrete ceiling for current approaches.
contributions.quote: We complement our real-world tasks with carefully controlled synthetic datasets at different complexity levels, enabling direct evaluation of how well models can recover known ground-truth hypotheses and demonstrating substantial room for improvement … as task difficulty increases, performance significantly drops, with best models and methods only recovering 38.8% of the ground-truth hypotheses.

contributions.statement: SafeScientist — an AI scientist framework with multi-layered safety mechanisms (prompt monitoring, agent-collaboration monitoring, tool-use monitoring, and an ethical reviewer) that proactively refuses high-risk or ethically inappropriate tasks while maintaining scientific output quality.
contributions.quote: We propose SafeScientist, an AI scientist framework integrating proactive prompt monitoring, agent collaboration oversight, tool-use constraints, and ethical review to ensure safety and ethical compliance.

contributions.statement: SciSafetyBench — a benchmark for evaluating AI safety in scientific contexts, comprising 240 high-risk discovery tasks across 6 scientific domains and 120 tool-specific risk scenarios covering 30 representative scientific tools.
contributions.quote: We introduce SciSafetyBench, a benchmark with 240 high-risk discovery tasks and 120 tool-specific risk tasks across six scientific domains for evaluating AI scientist safety.

contributions.statement: A suite of adversarial attack methods targeting AI scientist pipelines — including query injection, malicious discussion agents, and malicious experiment instructors — used to rigorously validate the robustness of safety defenses in multi-agent scientific environments.
contributions.quote: We implement diverse adversarial attacks to rigorously validate the robustness and effectiveness of SafeScientist and SciSafetyBench.

contributions.statement: Integrating multi-layered defenses into an AI scientist framework yields a 35% improvement in safety performance over traditional AI scientist frameworks without degrading scientific output quality.
contributions.quote: Extensive experiments demonstrate that SafeScientist significantly improves safety performance by 35% compared to traditional AI scientist frameworks, without compromising scientific output quality.

contributions.statement: Robin — a multi-agent system integrating literature search agents (Crow for concise reviews, Falcon for deep reviews) with an experimental data analysis agent (Finch) — automates hypothesis generation, experimental design, result interpretation, and iterative hypothesis refinement in a single continuous workflow, constituting the first end-to-end AI system for autonomous scientific discovery within a lab-in-the-loop framework.
contributions.quote: we introduce Robin, the first multi-agent system capable of fully automating the key intellectual steps of the scientific process. By integrating literature search agents with data analysis agents, Robin can generate hypotheses, propose experiments, interpret experimental results, and generate updated hypotheses, achieving a semi-autonomous approach to scientific discovery

contributions.statement: Ripasudil, a clinically approved rho kinase (ROCK) inhibitor, enhances retinal pigment epithelium phagocytosis and is a validated therapeutic candidate for dry age-related macular degeneration — a repurposing opportunity not previously proposed in the literature.
contributions.quote: Robin proposed enhancing retinal pigment epithelium phagocytosis as a therapeutic strategy, and identified and validated a promising therapeutic candidate, ripasudil. Ripasudil is a clinically-used rho kinase (ROCK) inhibitor that has never previously been proposed for treating dAMD

contributions.statement: ABCA1, a critical lipid efflux pump, is identified as a possible novel molecular target in dry age-related macular degeneration, revealed through AI-driven analysis of a follow-up RNA-seq experiment examining the mechanism of ripasudil-induced upregulation of phagocytosis.
contributions.quote: Robin then proposed and analyzed a follow-up RNA-seq experiment, which revealed upregulation of ABCA1, a critical lipid efflux pump and possible novel target

contributions.statement: DN-Hypo-Pipeline — an LLM-driven workflow that identifies underlying laws, theories, and principles from a paper's explanandum and reconstructs novel, yet-to-be-verified scientific hypotheses from existing literature using scientific explanations as prior knowledge.
contributions.quote: We introduce DN-Hypo-Pipeline, an AI-powered workflow based on large language models, designed to support structured scientific thinking and hypothesis generation by leveraging scientific explanations as prior knowledge.

contributions.statement: Empirical validation showing that structured hypothesis generation via scientific explanations outperforms direct LLM generation methods, as assessed by both LLM-as-judge and human expert evaluation.
contributions.quote: Statistical inference, supported by both LLM-as-judge assessment and human expert evaluation, demonstrates that our pipeline is more effective than direct generation methods.

contributions.statement: Novel algorithms derived from LLM-generated hypotheses that outperform baseline models from highly cited data science papers, providing end-to-end experimental validation of AI-generated scientific hypotheses.
contributions.quote: we validated the two highest-scoring generated hypotheses by developing corresponding novel algorithms, which outperformed the baseline models presented in the original papers.

contributions.statement: A theoretical framework that reveals a fundamental structure of theory-guided data science modeling and generalizes it to a broader class of scientific domains.
contributions.quote: DN-Hypo-Pipeline provides a theoretical framework that not only encompasses theory-guided data science modeling methods but also reveals a more fundamental structure of the modeling process. Moreover, this approach is essentially a generalization of theory-guided modeling, offering potential for extension to other domains and across a broader range of scientific disciplines.

contributions.statement: WebThinker — a deep research agent that enables large reasoning models to autonomously search the web, navigate web pages, and draft research reports within their continuous reasoning process, outperforming existing open-source agents and strong proprietary systems (Grok3, Gemini 2.0) on complex reasoning benchmarks (GPQA, GAIA, WebWalkerQA, HLE) and scientific report generation.
contributions.quote: we propose WebThinker, a deep research agent that empowers LRMs to autonomously search the web, navigate among web pages, and draft reports during the reasoning process

contributions.statement: Deep Web Explorer — a module that allows reasoning models to dynamically search, navigate web pages by clicking interactive elements such as links and buttons, and extract relevant information, supporting iterative follow-up searches and deep link traversal to close knowledge gaps encountered during reasoning.
contributions.quote: we design a Deep Web Explorer module that enables LRMs to search, navigate pages by clicking interactive elements (like links or buttons), and extract relevant information. Based on the current query's search results, the LRM can initiate follow-up searches and traverse deeper links until it collects all relevant information

contributions.statement: Autonomous Think-Search-and-Draft — a strategy that seamlessly interleaves reasoning, web information gathering, and section-by-section report drafting in real time, allowing models to write and refine report chapters concurrently with thinking and searching rather than only after all retrieval is complete.
contributions.quote: we introduce an Autonomous Think-Search-and-Draft strategy that deeply integrates report writing with the reasoning and search processes of LRMs. Rather than generating the entire report all at once after searching, our approach enables the model to draft and seek necessary knowledge in real-time

contributions.statement: An RL-based training strategy using iterative online Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) over large-scale sampled reasoning trajectories improves large reasoning models' ability to utilize research tools effectively for end-to-end complex task performance.
contributions.quote: we develop RL-based training strategies to optimize end-to-end task performance. We leverage the LRM equipped with the research tools to sample large-scale reasoning trajectories from complex tasks. Based on the accuracy of reasoning, tool usage, and final outputs, we construct preference pairs for online DPO training. Through iterative, on-policy RL training, the model progressively improves its ability to perceive, reason, and interact with research tools effectively

contributions.statement: An agentic LLM pipeline for automated reproducibility assessment that receives a dataset, a focal statistical claim, and contextual information from an article, then independently writes and executes statistical code to reproduce published claims at scale.
contributions.quote: We develop an agentic LLM workflow in which the model receives the original dataset, a focal statistical claim, and experimentally varied contextual information from the article, and is tasked to independently write and execute statistical code to reproduce the claims.

contributions.statement: LLM-automated reanalysis matches the qualitative conclusions of original social and behavioral science studies in 96% of cases and recovers original effect sizes (within ±0.05 Cohen's d) in 41% of cases, exceeding human reanalysts who match qualitative conclusions in 74% of cases and recover effect sizes in 34% of cases.
contributions.quote: our LLM pipeline recovered the original effect sizes in 41% of studies using a 0.05 tolerance in Cohen's d. Further, our LLM pipeline reached the same qualitative conclusion as the original study in 96% of cases … For comparison, human reanalysts recovered the original effect sizes in 34% of studies and reached the same qualitative conclusion in 74% of cases.

contributions.statement: A preregistered evaluation corpus of 76 published empirical studies from psychology, political science, and economics with predefined claims, datasets, and standardized analysis templates for benchmarking automated reproducibility tools against both original reported results and human reanalysis distributions.
contributions.quote: Using published studies with predefined claims from the behavioral and social sciences, we compare LLM-generated analysis with the original findings and human reanalysis … we address the following research question: To what extent can LLM-generated analyses reproduce the statistical results and substantive conclusions of published studies?

contributions.statement: StatefulDiscovery — a scientific discovery framework that externalizes investigation state to coordinate frontier selection, evidence acquisition, and claim adjudication — couples exploration trajectory with claim status to enable evidence-calibrated claim formation in open-ended discovery.
contributions.quote: We introduce StatefulDiscovery, a discovery framework that externalizes investigation state and uses it to coordinate frontier selection, evidence acquisition, and claim adjudication.

contributions.statement: Structured hypotheses, local adjudication, and frontier control each contribute independently to the quality of evidence-calibrated claims produced in open-ended scientific discovery.
contributions.quote: Ablations indicate that structured hypotheses, local adjudication, and frontier control contribute to performance.

contributions.statement: Explicit, externalized discovery state can couple multi-round scientific exploration with evidence-calibrated claim formation, yielding more claims that are simultaneously well-supported and high-value compared to baselines without such state management.
contributions.quote: Compared with several baselines, StatefulDiscovery produces more claims overall judged to be both well-supported and high-value. Together, these results suggest that explicit discovery state can couple exploration with evidence-calibrated claim formation.

contributions.statement: SocSci-Repro-Bench — a benchmark of 221 reproduction tasks drawn from 54 systematically selected social science papers spanning political science, sociology, psychology, and communication across 13 substantive domains — isolates agent reproduction capacity by including only tasks verified as fully reproducible with available materials or demonstrably non-reproducible due to missing data.
contributions.quote: we introduce SocSci-Repro-Bench, a benchmark of 221 tasks spanning four disciplines and 13 substantive domains, constructed from studies whose results are either fully reproducible with available materials or demonstrably non-reproducible due to missing data, allowing us to isolate agents' reproduction capacity

contributions.statement: Frontier AI coding agents achieve high computational reproducibility on social science findings: Claude Code reaches 93.4% task-level and 78.0% paper-level accuracy, substantially outperforming Codex (62.1% / 35.8%) and considerably exceeding reproduction rates previously reported for general-purpose LLM-based agents on comparable benchmarks.
contributions.quote: we find that both can reproduce a large share of social science findings, with Claude Code substantially outperforming Codex. These reproduction rates considerably exceed those previously reported for general-purpose LLM-based agents on comparable reproducibility benchmarks

contributions.statement: Providing the original paper PDF alongside replication materials modestly improves AI agent reproduction accuracy but introduces systematic bias on tasks where reproduction is impossible due to missing data, suggesting paper access encourages confirmatory rather than independent analysis.
contributions.quote: Providing the original paper PDF alongside replication materials modestly improves performance but introduces bias on tasks where reproduction is impossible

contributions.statement: AI coding agents are susceptible to sycophancy through prompt framing: subtle confirmatory nudges cause agents to engage in specification search that prioritizes alignment with reported results over faithful execution of supplied code.
contributions.quote: We also show that agents can be nudged toward confirmatory specification search through subtle prompt framing

contributions.statement: MLEvolve — a self-evolving multi-agent framework that unifies progressive graph search, retrospective memory, and hierarchical adaptive code generation — enables end-to-end machine learning algorithm discovery over long-horizon iterative optimization horizons.
contributions.quote: We propose MLEvolve, a self-evolving multi-agent framework for end-to-end MLE tasks, which unifies progressive graph search, retrospective memory, and hierarchical adaptive code generation to support long-horizon iterative optimization.

contributions.statement: Progressive Monte Carlo Graph Search (MCGS) resolves inter-branch information isolation in tree-based search by adding graph-based cross-branch reference edges and applies an entropy-inspired progressive schedule that shifts search from broad exploration to focused exploitation over time.
contributions.quote: We introduce Progressive MCGS and Retrospective Memory for self-evolving optimization. Progressive MCGS resolves inter-branch isolation through graph-based cross-branch information flow and a progressive exploration schedule

contributions.statement: Retrospective Memory — pairing a curated cold-start domain knowledge base with a dynamic global memory — enables automatic accumulation and retrieval of task-specific experience throughout the search without requiring additional LLM calls.
contributions.quote: Retrospective Memory enables automatic experience accumulation and retrieval throughout the search.

contributions.statement: Decoupling strategic planning from code generation and selecting among full-rewrite, stepwise, and diff-based editing modes according to search state improves iteration efficiency and stability in long-horizon code optimization agents.
contributions.quote: we further decouple strategic planning from code generation with adaptive coding modes

contributions.statement: An LLM-based MLE agent achieves a 65.3% average medal rate on MLE-Bench within a 12-hour budget (half the standard runtime) and outperforms AlphaEvolve and AlphaEvolve-v2 on mathematical algorithm optimization tasks, demonstrating cross-domain generalization of graph-search-based self-evolving agents.
contributions.quote: Extensive experiments show that MLEvolve achieves a 65.3% average medal rate on MLE-Bench under a 12-hour budget, achieving the best among all existing methods, and further outperforms AlphaEvolve and AlphaEvolve-v2 on mathematical optimization tasks, demonstrating cross-domain generalization.

contributions.statement: Science Earth — a planet-scale scientific runtime in which any capability (simulation cluster, wet-lab robot, proof engine, single-cell pipeline) can connect to any other and collaboration structure emerges from the scientific question itself rather than from a central designer's foresight — shifts the organizing challenge in AI-native discovery from workflow design to open-ended connectivity.
contributions.quote: Science Earth is a planet-scale scientific runtime built on a different premise: any scientific capability—a simulation cluster, a wet-lab robot, a mathematical proof engine, a single-cell pipeline—can connect to any other, and the collaboration structure emerges from the scientific question itself rather than a central designer's foresight.

contributions.statement: The Emergent Agent Collaboration Network (EACN) protocol stack — built above A2A transport and MCP tool invocation — adds domain-directed discovery, competitive bidding, cross-regime adjudication of incompatible evidentiary standards, and reputation-weighted trust, enabling heterogeneous scientific capabilities to discover one another and negotiate task ownership without any prior knowledge of participants.
contributions.quote: Science Earth addresses this challenge through the Emergent Agent Collaboration Network (EACN), a concrete protocol stack above A2A (transport) and MCP (tool invocation). EACN adds the coordination layer that neither standard provides: domain-directed discovery (any registered capability becomes findable by domain), competitive bidding (capabilities self-select for tasks based on confidence and reputation), cross-regime adjudication (partial results from different validity standards are compared, and conflicts spawn new sub-tasks), and reputation-weighted trust (accumulated across interactions).

contributions.statement: Emergent task decomposition — the property whereby coupled agents returning partial results under mutually incompatible evidentiary standards surface new scientific sub-questions that no individual participant could have formulated — enables open-connectivity networks to generate their own research agenda rather than executing a pre-specified workflow.
contributions.quote: When coupled participants return partial results on the same problem under different standards of evidence, their comparison can surface questions that no individual participant could have formulated. We call this property emergent task decomposition.

contributions.statement: In a distributed multi-agent run on a trans-Pacific higher-order Kuramoto synchronization problem, agents autonomously identified and corrected a closure-ratio assumption in Ott–Antonsen analytic theory that fails outside the Lorentzian limit within thirty minutes, demonstrating self-correcting scientific reasoning without human intervention.
contributions.quote: In a trans-Pacific higher-order Kuramoto synchronization study, agents autonomously identified and corrected a closure-ratio assumption in Ott–Antonsen analytic theory that fails outside the Lorentzian limit, within thirty minutes.

contributions.statement: An eight-agent heterogeneous-capability run on the 4.88M-cell Kang 2024 pan-cancer single-cell atlas, operating for 64.9 hours under a single structural external instruction, produced three new result layers and anchored findings against an independent wet-lab study on an adjacent CCR8⁻TIGIT⁺ Treg subset, demonstrating long-horizon emergent scientific coupling across diverse agent types.
contributions.quote: In an eight-agent single-cell run on the 4.88M-cell Kang 2024 pan-cancer atlas, heterogeneous capabilities coupled over a 64.9-hour window with exactly one structural external instruction, producing three new layers of result and anchoring findings against an independent wet-lab study on an adjacent CCR8- TIGIT+ Treg subset.

contributions.statement: ForeSci — a temporally controlled benchmark of 500 forward-looking AI research decision tasks spanning four AI domains and four decision families (direction forecasting, bottleneck–opportunity discovery, strategic research planning, venue-conditioned positioning), each paired with a cutoff-aligned offline knowledge base and pre-cutoff backbones, with post-cutoff papers withheld during generation and used only for validation; the same construction pipeline supports fully prospective forecasting as new literature emerges.
contributions.quote: A temporally-controlled benchmark with 500 tasks across four AI domains and four decision families, paired with cutoff-aligned offline knowledge bases and pre-cutoff backbones; the same pipeline supports fully prospective forecasting beyond retrospective evaluation

contributions.statement: A multi-signal evaluation protocol for forward-looking research judgement that separately measures prediction factuality, future-target alignment, evidence traceability, and reviewer persuasiveness, validated against human experts.
contributions.quote: A multi-signal evaluation protocol separating factuality, future-target alignment, evidence traceability, and reviewer persuasiveness, validated against human experts.

contributions.statement: Across LLM research agents, agent-style methods improve evidence traceability and factual support task-conditionally, but exhibit a recurring evidence-decision decoupling failure mode in which agents cite relevant pre-cutoff evidence while forecasting the wrong research object, mis-assigning causal roles, or selecting the wrong intervention.
contributions.quote: A systematic evaluation and diagnostic audit of LLM research agents showing that agent-style methods improve traceability and factuality task-conditionally, and identifying a previously-unstudied failure mode—evidence-decision decoupling.

contributions.statement: A corpus of 37,802 AI-generated scientific ideas produced by four research-agent frameworks (zero-shot, AIScientist, ResearchAgent, AgentLaboratory) paired with six LLMs across citation-defined research areas in AI and machine learning, enabling systematic comparison of AI and human scientific ideation.
contributions.quote: Using four AI research-agent frameworks and six large language models, we generate 37,802 scientific ideas from shared seed literature across citation-defined research areas in AI and machine learning.

contributions.statement: AI-generated scientific ideas are substantially more concentrated in topic space than human-authored papers from the same research areas, indicating that scale of AI ideation does not translate to breadth of scientific exploration.
contributions.quote: AI-generated ideas are substantially more concentrated than human-authored papers from the same research areas.

contributions.statement: AI-generated ideas remain much closer to their seed literature than human follow-on work does, staying within the conceptual neighborhood of input papers rather than moving into new research territory.
contributions.quote: AI-generated ideas remain much closer to their starting literature than later human follow-on work does. Rather than moving substantially beyond the seed literature, AI-generated ideas largely stay within the conceptual neighborhood of the input seed papers.

contributions.statement: Papers most similar to AI-generated research ideas tend to receive fewer subsequent citations, suggesting AI agents preferentially occupy lower-impact regions of the scientific landscape.
contributions.quote: papers most similar to AI-generated ideas tend to receive lower subsequent citations.

contributions.statement: When AI-generated ideas diverge from prior work, the divergence arises primarily from recombining existing technical methods rather than from posing fundamentally new research questions.
contributions.quote: when AI-generated ideas differ from prior work, the differences arise primarily from recombining existing technical methods rather than introducing fundamentally new research questions.

contributions.statement: AutoScientists — a decentralized multi-agent system for long-running computational scientific experimentation in which agents interpret a shared experimental state, self-organize dynamically into teams around promising hypotheses, filter proposals through peer critique before committing experimental compute, and propagate successes and failures across teams to reduce redundant exploration — without any central orchestrator or pre-specified search decomposition.
contributions.quote: We introduce AutoScientists, a self-organizing agent team for long-horizon scientific experimentation. Unlike prior systems that rely on central coordinators, consensus-based discussion, or fixed decompositions of the search space, AutoScientists allows agents to independently interpret a shared experimental state and decide which hypotheses to pursue. Agents post proposals to a shared forum where peers critique and filter them before experiments run, allowing teams and experimental directions to emerge through interaction rather than external assignment.

contributions.statement: Decentralized self-organizing agent teams outperform single-agent and centrally-coordinated multi-agent baselines on long-horizon scientific experimentation: on BioML-Bench (24 biomedical ML tasks) AutoScientists achieves a mean leaderboard percentile of 74.4%, improving the strongest prior AI agent by +8.33%; on GPT training optimization it reaches a target validation bits-per-byte 1.9× faster and discovers 7 accepted improvements where the single-agent baseline finds none; and on ProteinGym supervised substitution it improves average Spearman correlation by +6.5% over the prior state of the art across all 217 assays.
contributions.quote: State-of-the-art performance across scientific domains, with sustained improvement during long-running experimental search. AutoScientists improves over prior agents on biomedical ML, protein fitness prediction, and language-model training optimization, and continues identifying productive modifications after single-agent baselines stop improving.

contributions.statement: An automated-discovery extension to the Kermut protein fitness model for ACE2–Spike binding prediction achieves a +12.5% improvement in Spearman correlation over the prior state-of-the-art model, and the same frozen recipe transfers across all 217 ProteinGym supervised substitution assays to yield a +6.5% gain in average Spearman correlation.
contributions.quote: On ProteinGym fitness prediction, AutoScientists discovers a method for ACE2-Spike binding that improves over the current state-of-the-art model by +12.5% in Spearman correlation. Applied without modification across all 217 ProteinGym assays, the same method improves over the prior state of the art by +6.5% (Spearman correlation).

contributions.statement: InnovatorBench — a benchmark of 20 end-to-end LLM research tasks spanning data construction, filtering, augmentation, loss design, reward design, and scaffold construction — enables realistic, multi-dimensional evaluation of AI research agents on correctness, performance, output quality, and uncertainty, rewarding innovation over simple replication.
contributions.quote: We introduce InnovatorBench, the first benchmark to systematically evaluate AI research agents on end-to-end LLM research tasks, spanning data construction, filtering, and augmentation, loss design, reward design, and scaffold construction under multiple dimensions.

contributions.statement: ResearchGym — a general, extensible research environment — supports long-duration and distributed experiments, asynchronous execution, snapshot saving and loading, and a broad action space (terminal commands, file operations, web search, browsing) for realistic agentic research workflows.
contributions.quote: We develop ResearchGym, a general and extensible research environment supporting long-duration and distributed experiments, asynchronous execution, snapshot saving and loading, and a broad action space for realistic research workflows.

contributions.statement: Frontier LLMs evaluated as ReAct agents on end-to-end LLM research tasks show promise on code-driven sub-tasks but consistently fail on fragile algorithm design and long-horizon decision making, exhibiting impatience, resource mismanagement, poor library selection, and template-based reasoning, requiring over 11 hours to reach best performance.
contributions.quote: We perform an empirical analysis of InnovatorBench across multiple leading LLMs, demonstrating its potential and weaknesses in handling real LLM research tasks.

contributions.statement: SciIntBench — an adversarial benchmark of 810 prompts (270 matched triplets) spanning ten responsible conduct of research (RCR) categories and three scientific domains, where each scenario is presented in Overt Adversarial, Covert Adversarial, and Benign variants to jointly evaluate misconduct refusal and helpfulness on legitimate requests.
contributions.quote: We introduce SciIntBench, a benchmark of 270 triplets (Overt Adversarial, Covert Adversarial, Benign; 810 prompts in total) across ten RCR categories and three scientific domains.

contributions.statement: LLM refusal of research misconduct is strongly framing-sensitive: models that reliably refuse overtly stated misconduct frequently comply when the identical violation is framed covertly, and are especially vulnerable when misconduct is presented as a pressure-driven shortcut.
contributions.quote: We show that LLM refusal of research misconduct is highly sensitive to framing: models that reliably refuse overt misconduct may comply when the same request is framed covertly.

contributions.statement: Across 16 commercial and open-weight LLMs evaluated with an LLM-as-a-judge pipeline validated against human annotators, newer model generations refuse misconduct more reliably than older ones but remain vulnerable to covert framings, with the weakest refusal boundaries observed in the transparency, plagiarism, and fabrication RCR categories.
contributions.quote: We evaluate 16 commercial and open-weight LLMs from six providers from 2024 to 2026 using an LLM-as-a-judge pipeline validated against human annotators, and find that newer models tend to refuse misconduct more reliably but remain vulnerable to covert framings.

contributions.statement: A three-phase synthetic environment generation pipeline — topic sampling, HuggingFace-grounded dataset proposal, and SWE-agent-compatible code generation — automatically produces diverse machine learning research tasks without human supervision.
contributions.quote: we provide a novel synthetic environment generation pipeline targeting machine learning agents. Our pipeline automatically synthesizes machine learning challenges compatible with the SWE-agent framework, covering topic sampling, dataset proposal, and code generation.

contributions.statement: A self-debugging verification loop that iteratively feeds execution errors back into starter-code generation increases the validity and yield of automatically synthesized ML tasks.
contributions.quote: verified for higher quality with a self-debugging loop … If there is an error during the execution, we collect the errors and feed them back to the model in step 3 (starter code generation) … The iterative debug process can continue at most times.

contributions.statement: Supervised fine-tuning small language models (Qwen3-4B and Qwen3-8B) on teacher-generated trajectories from synthetic ML tasks raises their performance on the MLGym benchmark by 9% and 12% respectively on the AUP metric.
contributions.quote: The student models trained with our synthetic tasks achieve improved performance on MLGym rasing the AUP metric by 9% for Qwen3-4B and and 12% for Qwen3-8B.

contributions.statement: SciAgentGym — a scalable, stateful interactive environment integrating 1,780 domain-specific tools across Physics, Chemistry, Biology, and Materials Science, with supporting infrastructure (filesystem, scientific databases, Python interpreter) for grounding LLM agents in multi-turn scientific tool-use reasoning.
contributions.quote: SciAgentGym: An extensible environment integrating 1,780 domain-specific tools across four scientific disciplines to ground agents in multi-turn reasoning.

contributions.statement: SciAgentBench — a tiered evaluation suite of 259 tasks and 1,134 sub-questions spanning elementary single-tool actions (L1) to long-horizon multi-step workflows (L3), designed to isolate where agents fail in complex scientific problem-solving.
contributions.quote: SciAgentBench: An evaluation suite spanning elementary actions to long-horizon workflows, designed to quantify the gap between tool availability and mastery.

contributions.statement: SciForge — a data synthesis method that models a scientific tool collection as a dependency graph, samples valid execution paths, and generates logic-aware training trajectories grounded in verified runtime traces, enabling smaller fine-tuned models to outperform much larger models on scientific tool-use tasks.
contributions.quote: SciForge: A graph-based data synthesis method that generates logic-aware training trajectories, enabling our 8B model to outperform 200B+ scale models.

contributions.statement: Fine-tuning an 8B model (SciAgent-8B) on execution-grounded, dependency-graph-sampled trajectories surpasses Qwen3-VL-235B-Instruct by +6.7% on scientific tool-use benchmarks and exhibits positive cross-domain transfer of scientific tool-use capabilities.
contributions.quote: By fine-tuning on these trajectories, our SciAgent-8B outperforms the significantly larger Qwen3-VL-235B-Instruct while exhibiting positive cross-domain transfer of scientific tool-use capabilities.

contributions.statement: Medical AI Scientist — an end-to-end agentic framework for autonomous clinical research comprising an Idea Proposer, Experimental Executor, and Manuscript Composer — is the first autonomous research system explicitly tailored to clinical medicine, operating across three modes (paper-based reproduction, literature-inspired innovation, and task-driven exploration) with progressively increasing autonomy.
contributions.quote: we introduce Medical AI Scientist, the first autonomous research framework tailored to clinical autonomous research... The framework operates under 3 research modes, namely paper-based reproduction, literature-inspired innovation, and task-driven exploration, each corresponding to a distinct level of automated scientific inquiry with progressively increasing autonomy.

contributions.statement: A clinician–engineer co-reasoning mechanism grounds autonomously generated medical research hypotheses in verifiable literature evidence, improving traceability and reducing hallucinations in clinical AI ideation.
contributions.quote: A clinician–engineer co-reasoning mechanism is incorporated into the idea generation process to explicitly ground each hypothesis in verifiable evidence and mitigate hallucinations.

contributions.statement: A hierarchical manuscript composition approach guided by a structured medical writing paradigm and embedded ethical review mechanisms produces evidence-grounded clinical research drafts that comply with medical publication policies on data provenance and ethics.
contributions.quote: A hierarchical Manuscript Composer transforms research outputs into coherent and evidence-grounded drafts through a structured medical writing paradigm with enhanced narrative logic and readability. It also embeds ethical review mechanisms that explicitly document data usage in compliance with medical publication policies.

contributions.statement: Med-AI Bench — a benchmark of 171 evaluation cases spanning 19 clinical tasks and 6 medical data modalities — provides a standardized framework for assessing automated medical research systems across the full research pipeline (idea generation, experimental execution, and manuscript quality).
contributions.quote: we introduce Med-AI Bench... This benchmark comprises 171 high-quality evaluation cases, organized around 19 distinct research tasks spanning 6 common medical data modalities... This design provides a systematic and unified framework for both qualitative and quantitative assessment of automated medical research systems across the full research pipeline.

contributions.statement: EvoScientist — a self-evolving multi-agent framework comprising a Researcher Agent, an Engineer Agent, and an Evolution Manager Agent with two persistent memory modules (ideation memory and experimentation memory) — continuously improves scientific idea quality and code execution reliability by learning from accumulated successes and failures across end-to-end scientific discovery tasks.
contributions.quote: We propose EvoScientist, a self-evolving multi-agent system with three specialized agents and two persistent memory modules, aiming to improve both the quality of generated research ideas and the reliability of code generation and execution.

contributions.statement: Three multi-agent self-evolution mechanisms — idea direction evolution, idea validation evolution, and experiment strategy evolution — enable an AI scientist system to learn from accumulated outcomes and failures and to continuously improve both idea generation and experiment execution over time.
contributions.quote: We introduce three multi-agent self-evolution mechanisms, namely idea direction evolution, idea validation evolution, and experiment strategy evolution, that enable EvoScientist to learn from accumulated outcomes and failures and to continuously improve both idea generation and experiment execution over time.

contributions.statement: A multi-agent evolving AI scientist system with persistent memory achieves higher novelty, feasibility, relevance, and clarity in scientific idea generation and higher code execution success rates compared to seven open-source and commercial state-of-the-art baselines, with all six end-to-end generated papers accepted to a peer-reviewed venue and two receiving awards.
contributions.quote: We provide empirical evidence that EvoScientist generates higher-quality ideas and achieves higher code execution success rates compared to strong open-source and commercial baselines.

contributions.statement: aiXiv — an open-access, multi-agent platform with API and MCP interfaces — enables both human and AI scientists to submit, review, and iteratively refine scientific proposals and papers within a unified, extensible ecosystem.
contributions.quote: We introduce aiXiv, the first extensible infrastructure that enables seamless collaboration between AI agents and human researchers for generating, refining, and disseminating scientific proposals and papers. The platform provides APIs and MCPs interfaces for uploading, retrieving, reviewing and discussing scientific proposals and papers.

contributions.statement: A closed-loop review pipeline featuring automatic retrieval-augmented evaluation, reviewer guidance, multi-voting publication decisions, and a five-stage prompt injection detection and defense mechanism for AI-reviewed scientific submissions.
contributions.quote: We develop a closed-loop review system for both proposals and papers, featuring automatic retrieval-augmented evaluation, reviewer guidance, and defense mechanisms against prompt injection.

contributions.statement: Curated datasets for benchmarking scientific proposal quality and evaluating AI-driven review effectiveness in the context of AI-generated research content.
contributions.quote: We also release curated datasets for benchmarking proposal quality and evaluating review effectiveness.

contributions.statement: Iterative AI-driven review-and-refine cycles yield measurable improvements in the quality of AI-generated research proposals and papers, with gains in proposal ranking, review helpfulness, and final paper quality.
contributions.quote: Through comprehensive experiments on real-world scientific topics, we show that our review-refine pipeline substantially improves the quality of AI-generated research content. Iterative reviews yield measurable gains in proposal ranking, review helpfulness, and final paper quality.

contributions.statement: OpenLens AI — a modular, fully automated research agent framework for health informatics — integrates specialized agents for literature review, data analysis, code generation, and manuscript preparation into an end-to-end pipeline that requires no human intervention.
contributions.quote: A modular agent architecture tailored to health informatics, featuring specialized agents for literature review, data analysis, code generation, and manuscript preparation.

contributions.statement: Vision-language feedback integrated into an automated research pipeline enables iterative evaluation and refinement of medical visualizations (plots, charts, curves), closing a gap left by general-purpose research agents.
contributions.quote: Integration of vision-language feedback mechanisms to evaluate and refine visual outputs, including plots, charts, and curves—addressing a critical gap in current research agents.

contributions.statement: A quality control framework embedded in an autonomous health informatics agent enforces methodological soundness, statistical reliability, and reproducibility, reducing the likelihood of misleading or hallucinated findings.
contributions.quote: A quality control framework that supports methodological soundness, statistical reliability, and reproducibility, reducing the likelihood of misleading results.

contributions.statement: End-to-end automation of the health informatics research pipeline — from initial ideation through literature review, data analysis, and code execution to publication-ready LaTeX manuscript generation — is achievable with transparent and traceable workflows.
contributions.quote: End-to-end automation of the health informatics research pipeline, from initial ideation to publication-ready LaTeX manuscripts.

contributions.statement: STELLA — a self-evolving multi-agent system for biomedical research whose capabilities grow autonomously through a dynamically updated Template Library of successful reasoning workflows and an expanding Tool Ocean of bioinformatics tools discovered and integrated by a dedicated Tool Creation Agent.
contributions.quote: Here, we introduce STELLA, a self-evolving AI agent designed to overcome these limitations. STELLA employs a multi-agent architecture that autonomously improves its own capabilities through two core mechanisms: an evolving Template Library for reasoning strategies and a dynamic Tool Ocean that expands as a Tool Creation Agent automatically discovers and integrates new bioinformatics tools.

contributions.statement: A Tool Creation Agent mechanism that autonomously identifies capability gaps, searches for relevant bioinformatics tools and APIs, and integrates them into a live tool repository, enabling an agent's toolset to expand at inference time without manual curation.
contributions.quote: the Tool Creation Agent can autonomously identify, test, and integrate new tools in response to the demands of a novel problem, ensuring its capabilities are never limited to a predefined set

contributions.statement: Self-evolving test-time scaling in biomedical AI: iteratively running a multi-agent system on the same benchmark with increasing computational trials nearly doubles accuracy (14 % → 26 % on Humanity's Last Exam: Biomedicine), demonstrating systematic performance improvement through accumulated reasoning experience.
contributions.quote: This improvement is substantial; with increased computational experience, STELLA's accuracy on HLE: Biomedicine benchmark almost doubles, rising from 14% to 26%

contributions.statement: State-of-the-art accuracy on biomedical question-answering benchmarks: ~26 % on Humanity's Last Exam: Biomedicine, ~54 % on LAB-Bench DBQA, and ~63 % on LAB-Bench LitQA, outperforming leading LLMs and specialized agents by up to 8 percentage points.
contributions.quote: STELLA consistently establishes a new state of the art, achieving top accuracy scores of approximately 26% on Humanity's Last Exam: Biomedicine, 54% on LAB-Bench: DBQA, and 63% on LAB-Bench: LitQA, outperforming the next-best models by up to 8 percentage points

contributions.statement: A benchmark of three controlled black-box reverse-engineering tasks — reconstructing list-mapping programs, formal languages, and math equations — designed to assess scientific discovery capabilities of LLMs under both passive observation and active intervention conditions.
contributions.quote: we formalize reverse-engineering as a core problem for assessing the scientific discovery capabilities of LLMs and design three black-box tasks that can be used in such assessment.

contributions.statement: Frontier LLMs fail to efficiently extract information from passively observed input-output data when reverse-engineering black-box systems, reaching a performance plateau that falls significantly short of Bayesian inference.
contributions.quote: We demonstrate empirically that frontier LLMs still struggle, relative to Bayesian inference, at reverse-engineering these black boxes when provided with only passive observations.

contributions.statement: Allowing LLMs to actively intervene — self-generating targeted queries to a black-box and observing the outputs — improves reverse-engineering performance and helps escape two failure modes: overcomplication (assuming false prior knowledge) and overlooking (failing to incorporate observations).
contributions.quote: We show that LLMs can perform interventions to obtain more informative data, and that effective intervention mitigates the failure modes of overcomplication and overlooking.

contributions.statement: The performance gain from LLM-generated interventions stems primarily from the act of generating those interventions (self-testing and refining beliefs) rather than from data quality alone: feeding one LLM's intervention data as passive observations to a second LLM degrades performance, paralleling findings on human active learning.
contributions.quote: We show that performance degrades when repurposing the LLM's intervention data as observations, pinpointing the mechanism behind the improvements it produces and highlighting a potential pitfall for exchanging knowledge among LLMs.

contributions.statement: El Agente is a cognitive architecture for LLM-based agents that integrates working memory, long-term memory, and an LLM reasoning core into a hierarchical cybernetic agent network, enabling hierarchical planning, long-term memory retrieval, and modular memory management for complex multi-step task execution.
contributions.quote: we introduce El Agente, a novel cognitive architecture for language agents, and apply it to automate complex computational and quantum chemistry tasks. El Agente employs a hierarchical network of specialized LLM-based agents, each equipped with an extensive list of available tools.

contributions.statement: El Agente Q is an LLM-based multi-agent system that dynamically generates and executes quantum chemistry workflows from natural language prompts, with capabilities including flexible task decomposition, adaptive tool selection, post-analysis, autonomous file handling, SLURM job submission, and in situ error debugging.
contributions.quote: we introduce El Agente Q, an LLM-based multi-agent system that dynamically generates and executes quantum chemistry workflows from natural language user prompts. The system is built on a novel cognitive architecture featuring a hierarchical memory framework that enables flexible task decomposition, adaptive tool selection, post-analysis, and autonomous file handling and submission.

contributions.statement: An LLM-based multi-agent quantum chemistry system achieves greater than 87% average task success across six university-level course exercises and two research case studies, with adaptive error recovery via in situ debugging.
contributions.quote: El Agente Q is benchmarked on six university-level course exercises and two case studies, demonstrating robust problem-solving performance (averaging >87% task success) and adaptive error handling through in situ debugging.

contributions.statement: Exporting agent action traces as executable code provides a transparency mechanism that lets human experts verify procedural correctness and refine dynamically generated computational workflows.
contributions.quote: A key feature is its ability to extract the action traces of the agents involved in its dynamic workflows, which can be exported as code to verify the correctness of the procedure or assist humans in refined workflow design.

contributions.statement: A standardized comparative benchmark evaluating eight free-version AI chatbots—ChatGPT, Claude, Copilot, DeepSeek, Gemini, Grok, Le Chat, and Perplexity—on the accuracy of academic bibliographic reference generation across five major knowledge domains (Health, Engineering, Experimental Sciences, Social Sciences, and Humanities), auditing 400 references against five formal criteria (authorship, year, title, source, and location).
contributions.quote: The novelty of this study lies in offering the first comparative analysis of eight AI chatbots specifically focused on their ability to generate bibliographic references for student academic work.

contributions.statement: Only 26.5% of bibliographic references generated by current AI chatbots are fully accurate, while 33.8% are partially correct and 39.8% are erroneous or entirely fabricated, revealing a critical reliability gap for academic use.
contributions.quote: The results show that only 26.5% of the references were fully correct, 33.8% partially correct, and 39.8% were either erroneous or entirely fabricated.

contributions.statement: Grok and DeepSeek are the only chatbots among eight tested that produce zero hallucinated bibliographic references, whereas Copilot, Perplexity, and Claude exhibit the highest hallucination rates.
contributions.quote: Grok and DeepSeek stood out as the only chatbots that did not generate false references, while Copilot, Perplexity, and Claude exhibited the highest hallucination rates.

contributions.statement: AI chatbots show a systematic bias toward generating book references over journal articles, yet journal article citations carry a significantly higher fabrication rate than book citations.
contributions.quote: the chatbots showed a greater tendency to generate book references over journal articles, although the latter had a significantly higher fabrication rate.

contributions.statement: Substantial overlap exists in the bibliographic sources recommended by different AI chatbot models, particularly among DeepSeek, Grok, Gemini, and ChatGPT, indicating convergent generative behavior across ostensibly independent systems.
contributions.quote: A high degree of overlap was also detected among the sources provided by several models, particularly between DeepSeek, Grok, Gemini, and ChatGPT.

contributions.statement: AstaBench — a benchmark suite of 2400+ problems spanning the full scientific discovery process and multiple scientific domains, including problems derived from real user requests to deployed AI research assistants, with a standardized task interface for general-purpose agents.
contributions.quote: we present AstaBench, a suite that provides a holistic measure of agentic ability to perform scientific research, comprising 2400+ problems spanning the entire scientific discovery process and multiple scientific domains, and including many problems inspired by actual user requests to deployed Asta agents

contributions.statement: The Asta Environment — the first agent evaluation environment providing production-grade scientific literature search tools that enable controlled, reproducible agent evaluation while isolating core agentic capabilities from information-access advantages.
contributions.quote: AstaBench includes the powerful Asta Environment, the first agent environment that enables controlled, reproducible evaluation with production-grade search tools for retrieving information from a large corpus of scientific literature

contributions.statement: The agent-eval Agents Evaluation Toolkit — an open-source toolkit for defining benchmark suites and leaderboards with time-invariant cost accounting using logged model usage, enabling rigorous confounder-controlled agent comparison.
contributions.quote: We also introduce the agent-eval Agents Evaluation Toolkit, which enables defining a benchmark suite and leaderboard with time-invariant cost accounting using model usages logged by Inspect

contributions.statement: The first agent leaderboard that explicitly accounts for confounding variables — including tools used and inference cost — when reporting and comparing agent performance.
contributions.quote: It's the first agent leaderboard to properly account for confounding variables such as the tools used by the agent and inference cost

contributions.statement: A comprehensive standardized suite of nine science-optimized Asta agent classes plus numerous baselines, enabling meaningful comparison of 57 agents across 22 agent architectures on scientific research tasks.
contributions.quote: we present the agent-baselines Agents Suite, the most comprehensive standardized agents suite, comprised of nine Asta agent classes that have been optimized for scientific research tasks, as well as numerous baselines

contributions.statement: Reinforcement Learning with Evolving Rubrics (RLER) — a training method where per-instance rubrics are initialized from search-retrieved evidence and co-evolve with the policy model by incorporating newly explored search results and contrastive model responses at each training step, providing on-policy, discriminative rewards for long-form tasks.
contributions.quote: We address this with Reinforcement Learning with Evolving Rubrics (RLER), where rubrics are constructed and maintained to co-evolve with the policy model during training. This allows the rubrics to incorporate newly explored information from search and contrasting model responses, enabling better fact checking and more discriminative on-policy feedback.

contributions.statement: DR Tulu-8B — the first fully open language model directly trained end-to-end for open-ended, long-form deep research, outperforming larger open deep research agents and matching proprietary systems at roughly 1000× lower cost per query.
contributions.quote: Using RLER, we develop Deep Research Tulu (DR Tulu-8B), the first fully open model that is directly trained for open-ended, long-form deep research.

contributions.statement: GeneticDiseasesQA — a challenging clinical deep research dataset requiring models to search for and synthesize evidence to assess the therapeutic eligibility of disease-causing genetic variants, with verifiable citation requirements.
contributions.quote: We further construct GeneticDiseasesQA, a challenging clinical deep research dataset that requires models to search for and synthesize supporting evidence to assess the therapeutic eligibility of disease-causing genetic variants.

contributions.statement: An end-to-end open-source deep research training stack — including data, code, models, an extensible deep research library (dr-agent-lib), and an evaluation suite supporting plug-and-play multi-tool search with infrastructure for asynchronous tool calls and scalable RL over long-horizon tool-use trajectories.
contributions.quote: We release all data, code, and models, along with an extensible deep research library (dr-agent-lib) and an evaluation suite supporting plug-and-play multi-tool search. This release provides an end-to-end training stack for deep research agents, including data and infrastructure for asynchronous tool calls and scalable RL over long-horizon tool-use trajectories.

contributions.statement: Training on open-ended deep research with RLER yields a 6.4–16.0 point gain over supervised fine-tuning alone and causes models to adaptively select among multiple search tools (e.g., paper search vs. web search) based on task domain rather than relying on a single hard-coded tool.
contributions.quote: Our analysis shows that RLER improves the model's ability to produce more comprehensive and in-depth long-form responses with accurate citations, yielding gains of 6.4–16.0 points on top of the finetuned model across the four benchmarks.
