Moreover, DR Tulu-8B learns to select appropriate search tools for each task, instead of relying on a single hard-coded search tool like in prior work.

contributions.statement: A large-scale benchmark evaluating LLM-based scientific agents across eight domains — spanning workflow execution to hypothesis-driven inquiry — through more than 25,000 agent runs, using two complementary lenses: systematic performance analysis and epistemological behavioral analysis of reasoning traces.
contributions.quote: we evaluate LLM-based scientific agents across eight domains, spanning workflow execution to hypothesis-driven inquiry, through more than 25,000 agent runs and two complementary lenses: (i) a systematic performance analysis that decomposes the contributions of the base model and the agent scaffold, and (ii) a behavioral analysis of the epistemological structure of agent reasoning

contributions.statement: Decomposing agent behavior into base-model and scaffold components reveals that the base model is the dominant driver of both task performance and epistemic behavior, accounting for 41.4% of explained variance versus only 1.5% for the scaffold.
contributions.quote: the base model is the primary determinant of both performance and behavior, accounting for 41.4% of explained variance versus 1.5% for the scaffold

contributions.statement: LLM-based scientific agents systematically fail to reason epistemically: evidence is ignored in 68% of reasoning traces, refutation-driven belief revision occurs in only 26%, and convergent multi-test evidence is rare — patterns that persist across both workflow-execution and hypothesis-driven tasks and even when near-complete successful trajectories are provided as context.
contributions.quote: evidence is ignored in 68% of traces, refutation-driven belief revision occurs in 26%, and convergent multi-test evidence is rare. The same reasoning pattern appears whether the agent executes a computational workflow or conducts hypothesis-driven inquiry. They persist even when agents receive near-complete successful reasoning trajectories as context

contributions.statement: Outcome-based evaluation of scientific agents cannot detect epistemic reasoning failures, and scaffold engineering alone is insufficient to repair them — progress requires targeting the reasoning process itself at the base-model training level.
contributions.quote: Outcome-based evaluation cannot detect these failures, and scaffold engineering alone cannot repair them. Until reasoning itself becomes a training target, the scientific knowledge produced by such agents cannot be justified by the process that generated it

contributions.statement: DeepResearch Bench — a benchmark of 100 PhD-level research tasks spanning 22 distinct fields, constructed by domain experts to reflect the real-world distribution of deep research demands derived from over 96,000 user queries — enables systematic end-to-end evaluation of Deep Research Agents.
contributions.quote: We present DeepResearch Bench, the first specialized benchmark for evaluating Deep Research Agents, constructed through a systematic process that reflects authentic user needs.

contributions.statement: RACE (Reference-based Adaptive Criteria-driven Evaluation with Dynamic Weighting) — a report-quality evaluation framework that dynamically generates task-specific criteria and dimension weights, then scores a target report relative to a high-quality reference — achieves strong alignment with human judgment for assessing long-form research reports across diverse domains.
contributions.quote: We further propose RACE and FACT, two novel evaluation frameworks that respectively assess the report generation quality and the information retrieval abilities of Deep Research Agents.

contributions.statement: FACT (Factual Abundance and Citation Trustworthiness) — a framework that evaluates information retrieval and collection capabilities of research agents by measuring effective citation count and overall citation accuracy — provides a robust, human-aligned approach for assessing the coverage and reliability of information gathered by LLM-based agents.
contributions.quote: The other framework is introduced to evaluate DRA's information retrieval and collection capabilities by assessing its effective citation count and overall citation accuracy.

contributions.statement: Comprehensive human studies confirm that RACE and FACT achieve strong alignment with human judgment, validating both frameworks as reliable automated evaluators for Deep Research Agents.
contributions.quote: We conduct comprehensive human studies to validate the reliability of our frameworks and publicly release the benchmark and evaluation protocols to foster future research.

contributions.statement: Three explicit regulatory feedback loops — interpretive update (classifying incoming evidence against the current mental model), deviation feedback (scoring search outcomes against planner expectations to route corrective strategies), and schema revision (restructuring concept labels, relations, and inquiry goals when accumulated feedback shows the current framing diverges from reality) — constitute the central control mechanisms for keeping a deep-research agent's evolving intermediate representation aligned with reality under pervasive uncertainty.
contributions.quote: We identify three feedback loops as central regulatory mechanisms for an agent's mental model under pervasive uncertainty.

contributions.statement: VeriTrace — a cognitive-graph deep-research framework in which nodes represent concepts under inquiry (each carrying acceptance criteria, accumulated findings, and quality history), edges encode inquiry relations, and three explicit regulatory loops (interpretive update via a graph manager, deviation feedback via a CR-AAP-inspired quality assessor, and schema revision via five structural operations) govern how the graph evolves — improves over the strongest matched baseline by 4.22 pp on DeepResearch Bench Insight and 5.9 pp Overall win rate on DeepConsult using matched Qwen3.5-27B backbones, and achieves the strongest reproducible open-source result on DeepResearch Bench with Config-DeepSeek.
contributions.quote: We present VeriTrace, a cognitive-graph implementation organised around concepts rather than sub-tasks, with evidence preserved under structural revision.

contributions.statement: Explicit regulatory loops in deep-research agents improve benchmark performance beyond what backbone scale alone can provide; systems that delegate regulation to implicit LLM reasoning show substantial performance drops when the backbone is held fixed, whereas systems with explicit loops do not exhibit the same scale dependency.
contributions.quote: Comprehensive experimental studies show that explicit regulatory loops improve deep research performance beyond backbone scale alone.

contributions.statement: GPT4-generated hypotheses about novel synergistic pairs of FDA-approved non-cancer drugs targeting the MCF7 breast cancer cell line are experimentally validated in the laboratory, with three out of twelve first-round combinations and three out of four second-round combinations achieving synergy scores above positive controls.
contributions.quote: In the first round of laboratory experiments, GPT4 succeeded in discovering three drug combinations (out of 12 tested) with synergy scores above the positive controls. GPT4 then generated new combinations based on its initial results, this generated three more combinations with positive synergy scores (out of four tested).

contributions.statement: An iterative human–LLM–laboratory workflow in which an LLM proposes drug-combination hypotheses, wet-lab experiments test them, and the LLM refines its proposals based on empirical feedback demonstrably improves the yield of synergistic hits in cancer drug discovery.
contributions.quote: GPT4 then generated new combinations based on its initial results, this generated three more combinations with positive synergy scores (out of four tested).

contributions.statement: LLM hallucinations — probabilistic outputs that deviate from strict inference over training text — can be productively exploited as a source of novel scientific hypotheses whose validity is objectively assessable through laboratory experiments.
contributions.quote: In science, some hallucinations may be useful: novel hypotheses whose validity may be tested by laboratory experiments. Here, we experimentally test the application of LLMs as a source of scientific hypotheses … We conclude that LLMs are a valuable source of scientific hypotheses.

contributions.statement: GALILEO — Generalizable Agentic Laboratory Intelligence for Learning, Experimentation, and Optimization — is a physically embodied AI scientist that couples an Observation-Thought-Action-Summary (OTAS) reasoning loop with robotic peptide synthesis and multimodal phenotyping to close the prediction-to-intervention gap in therapeutic peptide discovery for dynamic membrane systems.
contributions.quote: Here we present Generalizable Agentic Laboratory Intelligence for Learning, Experimentation, and Optimization (GALILEO) as a physically embodied AI scientist for closed-loop therapeutic peptide discovery in dynamic membrane systems. GALILEO is not a de novo peptide generator. Its molecular starting point is a clinically informed peptide prior, and its agentic action is retrieval, local editing, synthesis, quality control, phenotyping, and hypothesis updating under physical feedback.

contributions.statement: A clinically informed peptide prior (CPP) — constructed from over 6.9 million unique peptide-spectrum matches across 1,093 tumor proteomes spanning nine cancer types and scored by an abundance-aware gated-fusion model with survival, recurrence, and malignancy heads — provides an auditable, clinically anchored starting pool of 4,822 synthesizable peptide candidates for retrieval-and-editing-based discovery.
contributions.quote: The clinically informed peptide prior (CPP) was constructed from CPTAC peptide-spectrum-match (PSM) tumor proteomics across nine cancer types, with clinical metadata, sequence features, abundance profiles, cancer-type labels… the final CPP contained 4,822 clinically anchored candidates with parent protein, cancer context, outcome label, sequence score, and physicochemical metadata

contributions.statement: Living-system feedback from closed-loop peptide discovery experiments yields a transferable Amphiphilic Balance Grammar (ABG) — a set of extractable rules for designing membrane-protein blockers.
contributions.quote: living-system feedback can yield a transferable Amphiphilic Balance Grammar for membrane-protein blockade

contributions.statement: Peptide blockade of LRRC8C volume-regulated anion channels perturbs osmolyte/redox homeostasis, activates tumor-cell-intrinsic STING signaling, and promotes tumor-dependent T-cell activation.
contributions.quote: LRRC8C blockade, which couples osmolyte/redox stress to tumor-cell-intrinsic STING signaling and tumor-dependent T-cell activation

contributions.statement: Peptide blockade of SLC25A1 disrupts citrate-export metabolism, reduces extracellular acidification, and enhances CD8+ T-cell function by promoting antitumor immune permissiveness.
contributions.quote: SLC25A1 blockade, which collapses citrate-export metabolism, reduces extracellular acidification, and promotes antitumor immune permissiveness

contributions.statement: CARIBOU — a multi-agent framework that autonomously executes, troubleshoots, and refines bioinformatics workflows within institutional HPC environments using a Singularity/Apptainer-based backend that requires no outbound network connections, making it compatible with real-world cluster security policies.
contributions.quote: we present CARIBOU (Computational AI Research Interface for Bioinformatics, Omics, and Unifying Agents), a multi-agent framework designed for practical deployment within institutional research computing environments… All analyses are executed within reproducible computational environments compatible with Singularity/Apptainer-based high-performance computing (HPC) systems commonly used in academic research

contributions.statement: Researcher-modifiable agent blueprints that encode analytical roles, domain knowledge, and lab-specific workflow standards into specialized AI agents, enabling labs to update analytical judgment without model fine-tuning or pipeline rewriting.
contributions.quote: CARIBOU organizes specialized AI agents through researcher-modifiable blueprints that encode analytical roles, domain knowledge, and workflow guidance… CARIBOU represents agent behavior as researcher-modifiable blueprints, allowing lab-specific judgment and domain standards to be encoded and updated without model fine-tuning or pipeline rewriting

contributions.statement: A persistent shared biological analysis state, represented as a living AnnData object, maintained coherently across multiple specialized AI agents spanning different stages of a single-cell omics analysis.
contributions.quote: CARIBOU maintains a single persistent biological analysis state, represented as a living AnnData object, across interactions between multiple specialized AI agents responsible for different analytical tasks

contributions.statement: Empirical evaluation of an autonomous multi-agent bioinformatics system across unit-task benchmarks, a metadata reconstruction challenge spanning six public datasets, and two end-to-end scRNA-seq analyses using Allen Brain Atlas hippocampus and Tabula Sapiens large intestine datasets, with qualitative case studies of adaptive reasoning during live analysis execution.
contributions.quote: We evaluate CARIBOU across unit-task benchmarks, a metadata reconstruction challenge spanning six public datasets, and two end-to-end single-cell RNA-seq analyses using Allen Brain Atlas hippocampus and Tabula Sapiens large intestine datasets, alongside qualitative case studies demonstrating adaptive reasoning during analysis execution

contributions.statement: BiomniBench — a process-level evaluation framework that scores the full agent trajectory against expert-designed, task-specific rubrics across six dimensions (data handling, method selection, statistical rigor, biological interpretation, scientific reasoning, and source reliability) — addresses the dual failures of outcome-only benchmarks: reward hacking on memorized answers and unjust penalization of valid alternative analyses.
contributions.quote: We introduce BiomniBench, a process-level evaluation framework that scores the full agent trajectory against expert-designed, task-specific rubrics applied by an LLM judge, rather than scoring only the final result.

contributions.statement: BiomniBench-DA — 100 biomedical data-analysis tasks spanning 17 task types, 5 disease areas, and a general-biology category, each grounded in a Nature/Cell/Science paper and co-developed with an original author or a 5+ year domain expert — is the first process-level benchmark for LLM agents in real-world biomedical research.
contributions.quote: We therefore release BiomniBench-DA: 100 data analysis tasks curated from papers in Nature, Cell, Science, and similar journals, spanning 17 task types across 5 disease areas and a general-biology category. Each task is co-developed with either an original paper author or a domain expert with 5+ years of research experience.

contributions.statement: The choice of agent harness shifts biomedical data-analysis scores by more than the performance gap between successive frontier model generations, indicating that harness design is a stronger lever than model capability for current LLM agents.
contributions.quote: The agent harness shifts scores by more than the gap between successive model generations.

contributions.statement: Across frontier and open-weight LLMs evaluated on biomedical data analysis, agents reliably ground claims in real sources but consistently underperform on method selection, biological interpretation, and scientific reasoning, identifying these as the primary capability gaps in current systems.
contributions.quote: Dimension-level analysis reveals that agents reliably ground claims in real sources yet consistently fall short on method selection, biological interpretation, and scientific reasoning.

contributions.statement: FlowBench — a benchmark that decomposes agentic bioinformatics evaluation into four independently scored layers (planning, fault recovery, biological interpretation, and end-to-end output-fidelity) — exposes capability gaps that single-metric evaluations conflate.
contributions.quote: We introduce FlowBench, a benchmark that decomposes agentic bioinformatics performance into planning, fault recovery, biological interpretation, and end-to-end output-fidelity.

contributions.statement: FlowAgent — a modular, provider-agnostic agentic bioinformatics framework whose backbone model and scaffold components can be independently swapped or disabled across OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google Gemini providers on a shared harness — enables controlled attribution of performance to architecture versus model.
contributions.quote: We therefore built FlowAgent, a modular, provider-agnostic framework whose components can be selectively disabled and whose backbone model can be swapped across providers on a shared harness, and used it to evaluate 23 models from three main providers.

contributions.statement: Inferring an appropriate bioinformatics toolchain from biological intent alone is uniformly difficult across all model tiers, compressing 23 evaluated models into a narrow 44–57% pass-rate band, whereas generating a valid plan from a named toolchain is largely solved.
contributions.quote: generating a valid workflow plan from a named toolchain is largely solved, whereas inferring an appropriate toolchain from biological intent alone is uniformly difficult regardless of model tier, compressing all models into a narrow 44–57% pass-rate band.

contributions.statement: Dependency-structured workflow planning and a completeness-reflection step are the architectural components that drive plan quality, while adding a same-context validator-driven retry degrades structural correctness rather than improving it.
contributions.quote: ablation shows that the dependency-structured plan and a completeness-reflection step drive performance, while adding a same-context validator-driven retry makes structural quality worse.

contributions.statement: Fault recovery and data-grounded biological interpretation remain unsolved for current LLM agents: models frequently force clean exits that leave underlying data invalid, and reasoning-tier models are among the least reliable at recognising unrecoverable faults, while data-grounded interpretation consistently lags internal-knowledge recall.
contributions.quote: fault recovery and data-grounded interpretation remain unsolved. Models frequently propose fixes that force a clean exit while leaving the underlying data invalid, and data-grounded interpretation lags internal-knowledge recall by a consistent margin. Safety does not emerge from capability, and reasoning-tier models were among the least reliable at recognising unrecoverable faults.

contributions.statement: KBase Research Agent — a multi-agent system that combines a LangGraph state-machine for lifecycle orchestration with specialized CrewAI execution crews, grounded in a KBase-specific knowledge graph of the app catalog and retrieval over KBase documentation, to automate genome-assembly and annotation workflows and preserve results as reproducible KBase Narratives.
contributions.quote: We develop an agent framework grounded in KBase-specific knowledge sources, including retrieval over KBase documentation, tutorials and knowledge graph (KG) constructed from the KBase app catalog. … This hybrid architecture combines two agentic frameworks. At the highest level, we leverage LangGraph for flexible, stateful orchestration of the entire research lifecycle-from planning to execution and reporting. For the execution phase, we instantiate specialized, process-driven CrewAI teams.

contributions.statement: Automated KBase application selection, parameter configuration, and execution: given sequencing reads and a research objective, an agent system translates those inputs into fully parameterized, runnable KBase workflows whose outputs are automatically captured as provenance-preserving Narratives.
contributions.quote: We develop an agent system that translates research goals and input data into executable KBase workflows by selecting appropriate KBase applications, configuring their required parameters and inputs, and launching analyses automatically. Because these analyses are executed within KBase, the resulting workflows and outputs are preserved as KBase Narratives.

contributions.statement: End-to-end autonomous bioinformatics pipeline — spanning interactive goal-setting, metadata collection, workflow planning with human-approval checkpoints, automated execution, and draft manuscript generation in Microbial Resource Announcement format — requiring no human intervention between raw reads and a publishable result.
contributions.quote: The KBase Research Agent provides a complete solution that automates the full scientific workflow, from interactive user goal-setting and metadata collection, through workflow planning and validation, to automated execution and generation of draft manuscripts.

contributions.statement: A domain-specific evaluation framework, derived from expert peer-reviewed KBase Narratives used as ground truth, for assessing both the planning validity and execution reliability of automated bioinformatics workflow agents.
contributions.quote: To rigorously validate our system, we develop domain-specific eva[luation framework derived from expert, peer reviewed Narratives to assess both planning validity and execution reliability].

contributions.statement: Autonomous genome analysis of 100 previously uncharacterized bacterial isolates from the JGI IMG/M database — including read QC, assembly, GTDB-Tk taxonomic classification, annotation, reproducible Narratives, and draft manuscripts — produced without human intervention, demonstrating scalable end-to-end scientific workflow automation.
contributions.quote: We further apply the agent to 100 previously unanalyzed bacterial isolate genomes from the JGI IMG/M database, where it autonomously performed read quality control, genome assembly, taxonomic classification with GTDB-Tk, and downstream analysis producing annotated genomes, reproducible Narratives, and draft manuscripts without human intervention.

contributions.statement: SciHypo is a deep learning framework that generates novel scientific hypotheses by analyzing patterns, relationships, and gaps in scientific literature using GPT-based natural language processing.
contributions.quote: we introduce SciHypo, a deep learning framework leveraging scientific literature to generate fresh hypotheses across diverse fields. SciHypo employs advanced natural language processing, including Generative Pre-trained Transformer to analyze patterns, relationships, and gaps in research.

contributions.statement: Filter search algorithms improve the relevance and quality of automatically generated scientific hypotheses.
contributions.quote: Filter search algorithms ensure the production of relevant hypotheses.

contributions.statement: Deep learning-based hypothesis generation systems can adapt across diverse scientific disciplines, enabling transdisciplinary research discovery.
contributions.quote: A key strength is SciHypo's adaptability across scientific disciplines, enabling transdisciplinary hypothesis generation.

contributions.statement: Language models can uncover latent connections in scientific literature to propose novel research trajectories.
contributions.quote: This allows the model to uncover latent connections and propose novel research trajectories.

contributions.statement: QUASAR — a universal autonomous system that orchestrates complex multiscale atomistic workflows across density functional theory, machine learning potentials, molecular dynamics, and Monte Carlo simulations using LLM-driven reasoning for parameter selection and workflow adaptation.
contributions.quote: we introduce QUASAR, a universal autonomous system for atomistic simulations designed to facilitate production-grade scientific discovery. QUASAR autonomously orchestrates complex multiscale workflows across diverse methods, including density functional theory (DFT), machine learning potentials, molecular dynamics (MD), and Monte Carlo (MC) simulations. The system incorporates robust mechanisms for adaptive planning, context-efficient memory management, and hybrid knowledge retrieval to navigate real-world research scenarios without human intervention.

contributions.statement: Double-pass adaptive planning — an initial plan generation followed by a second-pass review that explicitly checks for missing scientific elements before execution.
contributions.quote: we adopted a double-pass planning mechanism: an initial plan produced by the Strategist is subjected to a second-pass review that explicitly checks for missing elements before execution.

contributions.statement: Dynamic tool integration for agentic systems — an extensible architecture that enables flexible integration of new computational tools without requiring major architectural re-engineering or new specialized agent decomposition.
contributions.quote: QUASAR is designed to support dynamic tool integration. Many existing agentic systems rely on hard-coded agent hierarchies and tightly scoped domain-specific tools, which can make their architectures rigid and difficult to extend. Adding new tools to these systems often requires major architectural re-engineering, introducing new specialized agents and functions. QUASAR significantly lowers the barrier to extensibility.

contributions.statement: Three-tiered atomistic simulation benchmark — an evaluation framework ranging from routine computational tasks to frontier research challenges, including photocatalyst screening and novel material assessment, for assessing autonomous agentic systems in computational chemistry.
contributions.quote: We benchmark QUASAR against a series of three-tiered tasks, progressing from routine tasks to frontier research challenges, such as photocatalyst screening and novel material assessment.

contributions.statement: Fine-tuning GPT-3 on social psychology literature generates research hypotheses rated by domain experts as comparable to human-generated hypotheses in clarity, originality, and impact.
contributions.quote: In the first approach, we fine-tuned the third-generation generative pre-trained transformer (GPT-3) language model on thousands of abstracts published in more than 50 social psychology journals in the past 55 years as well as on preprint repositories (PsyArXiv). Social psychology experts rated model- and human-generated hypotheses similarly on the dimensions of clarity, originality, and impact.

contributions.statement: GPT-4 generates social psychology research hypotheses rated by domain experts as higher in quality than human-generated hypotheses on clarity, originality, impact, plausibility, and relevance.
contributions.quote: In the second approach, without fine-tuning, we generated hypotheses using GPT-4 and found that social psychology experts rated these generated hypotheses as higher in quality than human-generated hypotheses on dimensions of clarity, originality, impact, plausibility, and relevance.

contributions.statement: ReviewRL, a reinforcement learning framework for generating scientific paper reviews that integrates retrieval-augmented context generation via ArXiv-MCP, supervised fine-tuning, and RL optimization with composite rewards.
contributions.quote: We introduce ReviewRL, a reinforcement learning framework for generating comprehensive and factually grounded scientific paper reviews. Our approach combines: (1) an ArXiv-MCP retrieval-augmented context generation pipeline that incorporates relevant scientific literature, (2) supervised fine-tuning that establishes foundational reviewing capabilities, and (3) a reinforcement learning procedure with a composite reward function that jointly enhances review quality and rating accuracy.

contributions.statement: A composite reward function combining rule-based metrics and judge-model-based evaluations for reinforcement learning in scientific review generation, addressing limitations of purely rule-based reward approaches.
contributions.quote: we design a comprehensive reward system incorporating both rule-based metrics and judge-model-based evaluations, effectively mitigating the limitations of purely rule-based methods.

contributions.statement: An action discovery agent method that systematically mines essential tools, databases, and protocols from biomedical literature to construct a unified biomedical action space across 25 domains.
contributions.quote: Biomni first employs an action discovery agent to create the first unified agentic environment – mining essential tools, databases, and protocols from tens of thousands of publications across 25 biomedical domains.

contributions.statement: Biomni-E1 — a unified biomedical agentic environment integrating 150 specialized tools, 105 software packages, and 59 databases across 25 biomedical domains.
contributions.quote: Biomni-E1 includes 150 specialized biomedical tools, 105 software packages, and 59 databases.

contributions.statement: A generalist agent architecture combining LLM reasoning, retrieval-augmented planning, and code-based execution to enable dynamic composition of complex biomedical workflows without predefined templates.
contributions.quote: Biomni features a generalist agentic architecture that integrates large language model (LLM) reasoning with retrieval-augmented planning and code-based execution, enabling it to dynamically compose and carry out complex biomedical workflows – entirely without relying on predefined templates or rigid task flows.

contributions.statement: Strong generalization of biomedical AI agents across heterogeneous tasks including causal gene prioritization, drug repurposing, rare disease diagnosis, microbiome analysis, and molecular cloning without task-specific prompt tuning.
contributions.quote: Systematic benchmarking demonstrates that Biomni achieves strong generalization across heterogeneous biomedical tasks – including causal gene prioritization, drug repurposing, rare disease diagnosis, microbiome analysis, and molecular cloning – without any task-specific prompt tuning.

contributions.statement: Autonomous generation of experimentally testable laboratory protocols through multi-modal biomedical dataset interpretation and analysis.
contributions.quote: Real-world case studies further showcase Biomni's ability to interpret complex, multi-modal biomedical datasets and autonomously generate experimentally testable protocols.

contributions.statement: ResearcherBench — the first benchmark designed to evaluate Deep AI Research Systems (DARS) on frontier scientific questions, assessing systems' ability to provide meaningful insights on unsolved, cutting-edge problems rather than just retrieving and summarizing established knowledge.
contributions.quote: To address this critical gap in evaluation methodology, we introduce ResearcherBench, the first benchmark specifically designed to evaluate DARS capabilities on frontier scientific questions

contributions.statement: A curated dataset of 65 frontier research questions sourced from authentic scientific scenarios including laboratory discussions and interviews with leading AI researchers, spanning 35 distinct AI subjects and categorized into technical details, literature review, and open consulting types.
contributions.quote: We present a carefully curated dataset of 65 high-quality research questions sourced from authentic frontier scenarios, including laboratory discussions, interviews with leading AI researchers, and active scientific forums. These questions span 35 distinct AI research subjects and are categorized into three distinct types.

contributions.statement: A dual evaluation framework combining rubric assessment using expert-designed criteria to evaluate insight quality with factual assessment measuring citation accuracy (faithfulness) and coverage (groundedness).
contributions.quote: Our assessment methodology combines rubric assessment with factual assessment. The rubric assessment employs domain expert-crafted evaluation criteria tailored to each specific question, ensuring alignment with human-anchored high-value insights. The factual assessment framework introduces two complementary metrics: faithfulness score and groundedness score, to evaluate the overall factual accuracy and coverage of generated content.

contributions.statement: OpenAI Deep Research and Gemini Deep Research significantly outperform other DARS systems on frontier questions, with particular strength in open-ended consulting tasks, while exhibiting a paradoxical pattern of high citation accuracy despite limited coverage.
contributions.quote: Results show that OpenAI Deep Research and Gemini Deep Research significantly outperform other systems, with particular strength in open-ended consulting questions... The evaluation also uncovers a paradoxical 'high faithfulness, low groundedness' pattern, and shows that high citation coverage does not necessarily equate to superior insight quality.

contributions.statement: El Agente Gráfico — a single-agent framework embedding LLM-driven decision-making in type-safe execution graphs and knowledge-graph persistence — manages context through typed symbolic identifiers for scientific automation.
contributions.quote: In this work, we present El Agente Gráfico, a single-agent framework that embeds LLM-driven decision-making within a type-safe execution environment and dynamic knowledge graphs for external persistence.

contributions.statement: Object-graph mapping represents computational state as typed Python objects, enabling persistent storage, consistent tool orchestration, and provenance tracking across heterogeneous scientific workflows.
contributions.quote: Central to our approach is a structured abstraction of scientific concepts and an object-graph mapper that represents computational state as typed Python objects, stored either in memory or persisted in an external knowledge graph. This design enables context management through typed symbolic identifiers rather than raw text, thereby ensuring consistency, supporting provenance tracking, and enabling efficient tool orchestration.

contributions.statement: ConceptualAtoms — a unified abstraction layer for molecular and periodic systems — enables zero-copy state transfer and on-the-fly transformations across heterogeneous computational packages.
contributions.quote: Across heterogeneous packages, the ConceptualAtoms class provides a unified in-memory interface for molecular and periodic systems, including charge/multiplicity verification. Package-specific input/output classes compose ConceptualAtoms for state representation, enabling on-the-fly transformations (e.g., conformer sampling followed by electronic-structure calculations) while maintaining direct Python object references for zero-copy state transfer.

contributions.statement: Single LLM-driven agents coupled with type-safe execution engines robustly perform complex, multi-step, and parallel computations in quantum chemistry and materials science applications.
contributions.quote: We evaluate the system by developing an automated benchmarking framework across a suite of university-level quantum chemistry tasks previously evaluated on a multi-agent system, demonstrating that a single agent, when coupled to a reliable execution engine, can robustly perform complex, multi-step, and parallel computations.

contributions.statement: A spectrum of integration methods for combining experts, LLMs, and data-driven approaches in causal hypothesis generation, ranging from purely knowledge-driven to purely data-driven, with varying degrees of interaction between sources.
contributions.quote: we introduce a spectrum of integration methods for experts, LLMs, and data-driven approaches. We review existing approaches for causal hypothesis generation and classify them within this spectrum.

contributions.statement: A framework for characterizing LLMs as probabilistic imperfect experts along dimensions of reliability, consistency, uncertainty, and content versus reasoning capabilities.
contributions.quote: Characterizing imperfect experts along dimensions such as (1) reliability, (2) consistency, (3) uncertainty, and (4) content vs. reasoning are emphasized for developing adaptable methods.

contributions.statement: A hybrid causal discovery algorithm combining LLM-based and data-driven approaches for deeper integration in identifying causal structures.
contributions.quote: As an example, our hybrid (LLM + data) causal discovery algorithm illustrates ways for deeper integration.

contributions.statement: CoDaS — A multi-agent framework for biomarker discovery from wearable sensor data, structured as an iterative six-phase process combining hypothesis generation, statistical and machine learning analysis, adversarial validation, mechanistic reasoning, and literature research with human oversight.
contributions.quote: We introduce CoDaS (AI Co-Data-Scientist), a multi-agent system that structures biomarker discovery as an iterative process combining hypothesis generation, statistical analysis, adversarial validation, and literature-grounded reasoning with human oversight using large-scale wearable datasets.

contributions.statement: Digital biomarker validation framework operationalizing replication, stability, robustness, and discriminative power via 11 checks including permutation testing, bootstrap confidence intervals, subgroup consistency analysis, and methodological triangulation.
contributions.quote: Each candidate was subjected to a validation battery operationalizing four complementary dimensions: replication, stability, robustness, and discriminative power via 11 checks (including permutation testing, bootstrap confidence intervals, subgroup consistency, and methodological triangulation), consistent with emerging digital biomarker standards.

contributions.statement: Sleep variability features (duration and onset variability) are circadian instability-related biomarkers associated with depression severity across independent wearable datasets.
contributions.quote: Across two independent depression cohorts, CoDaS surfaced circadian instability-related features in both datasets, reflected in sleep duration variability (DWB, ρ= 0.252, p < 0.001) and sleep onset variability (GLOBEM, ρ= 0.126, p < 0.001).

contributions.statement: A wearable-derived cardiovascular fitness index (steps/resting heart rate ratio) is associated with insulin resistance and metabolic outcomes.
contributions.quote: In a metabolic cohort, CoDaS derived a cardiovascular fitness index (steps/resting heart rate; ρ= -0.374, p < 0.001), and recovered established clinical associations, including the hepatic function ratio (AST/ALT; ρ= -0.375, p < 0.001), a known correlate of insulin resistance.

contributions.statement: Iteris — an explore-plan-execute agentic research loop coordinating multiple research modes (conjecture formulation, numerical experimentation, proof construction, and route review) for open problems in computational mathematics.
contributions.quote: We describe the Iteris system: an explore–plan–execute agentic loop system for distinct research modes in computational mathematics.

contributions.statement: A fixed-parameter phase diagram characterizing the asymptotic cost comparison between randomized coordinate descent and conjugate gradient on power-law spectra, identifying regimes where each method dominates in the normalized cost ratio.
contributions.quote: We establish a fixed-parameter phase diagram for the CG-versus-RCD comparison on power-law spectra, giving asymptotic rates for the normalized cost ratio between RCD and CG (Theorem 1).

contributions.statement: A family of low-coherence orthonormal-row matrices demonstrating that QR factorization with column pivoting can fail to select well-conditioned submatrices, refuting a potential positive result.
contributions.quote: We answer the QRCP orthonormal-selection question in the negative by constructing a low-coherence orthonormal-row counterexample family (Theorem 2).

contributions.statement: Co-Scientist is a structured scientific thinking engine that synthesizes scientific literature, generates novel hypotheses conditioned on research objectives and prior evidence, and proposes experimental protocols for verification.
contributions.quote: we introduce Co-Scientist, a multi-agent AI system built on Gemini for structured scientific thinking and hypothesis generation... it formulates demonstrably novel research hypotheses for experimental verification

contributions.statement: A multi-agent architecture with asynchronous task execution framework flexibly allocates computational resources for scientific reasoning, enabling dynamic scaling of test-time compute.
contributions.quote: a multi-agent architecture with an asynchronous task execution framework for flexible compute scaling

contributions.statement: Tournament-based evolution using self-play scientific debate iteratively compares, ranks, and refines hypotheses to improve their quality.
contributions.quote: a tournament evolution process for self-improving hypotheses generation

contributions.statement: Scaling test-time compute through multi-agent self-play and tournament evolution improves hypothesis quality for complex scientific problems.
contributions.quote: Automated evaluations show continued benefits of test-time compute scaling, improving hypothesis quality over time

contributions.statement: An interactive system design enabling scientists to specify research objectives in natural language and actively steer hypothesis generation through conversational feedback.
contributions.quote: Co-Scientist is purpose-built for a 'scientist-in-the-loop' collaborative paradigm... Scientists can specify their research goals in simple natural language... They can also actively interact with, and steer the system

contributions.statement: FlamePilot — an LLM agent system for combustion modeling that autonomously executes self-corrective CFD workflows while extracting guidance from scientific literature and maintaining verifiable, interpretable reasoning.
contributions.quote: we introduce FlamePilot, an LLM agent designed to empower combustion modeling research through automated and self-corrective CFD workflows.

contributions.statement: Atomic tools architecture for CFD agents — using granular basic tools (file I/O, bash execution, grep search) instead of monolithic functions enables robust setup and execution of complex simulations with flexible error correction.
contributions.quote: FlamePilot addresses this through a foundation of atomic tools following established coding agent paradigms, including file read/write, directory listing, grep search, and bash execution. This granular toolset enables dynamic composition for complex CFD tasks

contributions.statement: Paper analysis skill that automatically extracts critical CFD parameters (geometry, mesh configuration, model selections, tuning parameters) from academic PDFs by converting them to structured markdown.
contributions.quote: A dedicated paper analysis skill instructs the agent to process academic PDFs into markdown format and extract critical CFD parameters. This includes geometry and mesh details, model selections, and specific tuning parameters documented in prior studies.

contributions.statement: Perfect executability (1.0 score) on FoamBench-Advanced benchmark with 0.438 success rate, surpassing prior best reported CFD agent scores of 0.625 executability and 0.250 success rate.
contributions.quote: FlamePilot achieved a perfect executability score (Mexec), successfully producing executable OpenFOAM cases across all 16 tests. This represents a decisive improvement over the previously best-reported benchmark score of 0.625

contributions.statement: Modular skills system — a framework where domain expertise is organized into self-contained units (instructions, scripts, resources) that dynamically augment agent capabilities across different simulation frameworks without workflow-specific tool redesign.
contributions.quote: FlamePilot achieves this through a structured 'skills' system inspired by Claude's Agent Skills, which dynamically augments the agent's capabilities with specialized expertise. The system organizes knowledge into modular units containing instructions, scripts, and resources that the agent discovers and loads on-demand.

contributions.statement: DeepReview — a multi-stage structured framework for automated paper review that emulates expert reviewers through novelty assessment, multi-dimensional evaluation criteria, structured analysis, literature retrieval, and reliability verification
contributions.quote: we introduce DeepReview, a multi-stage framework designed to emulate expert reviewers by incorporating structured analysis, literature retrieval, and evidence-based argumentation

contributions.statement: DeepReview-13K — a dataset of 13,378 research papers with fine-grained structured intermediate review steps and final assessments capturing expert paper review reasoning processes
contributions.quote: The resulting dataset, DeepReview-13K, consists of raw research papers, structured intermediate review steps, and final assessments.

contributions.statement: DeepReviewer-14B — a 14-billion parameter model trained on structured review reasoning that outperforms larger models like CycleReviewer-70B, achieves 80% win rates against GPT-o1 and DeepSeek-R1, and offers multiple inference modes for balancing efficiency and quality
contributions.quote: we train DeepReviewer-14B, a model that offers three inference modes – Fast, Standard, and Best – allowing users to balance efficiency and response quality

contributions.statement: DeepReview-Bench — a comprehensive benchmark with 1,286 samples for evaluating paper review systems on both quantitative aspects (rating prediction, ranking, paper selection) and qualitative review generation quality
contributions.quote: We further construct DeepReview-Bench, a comprehensive benchmark containing 1.2K samples, which evaluates both quantitative aspects (rating prediction, quality ranking, and paper selection) and qualitative review generation through LLM-based assessment.

contributions.statement: BioML-bench enables evaluation of AI agents on end-to-end biomedical ML tasks across four domains (protein engineering, single-cell omics, biomedical imaging, drug discovery), where agents must implement complete ML pipelines from task description to validated predictions.
contributions.quote: We introduce BioML-bench, the first benchmarking suite for evaluating AI agents on end-to-end biomedical ML tasks. BioML-bench spans four domains (protein engineering, single-cell omics, biomedical imaging, and drug discovery) with tasks that require agents to parse a task description, build a pipeline, implement models, and submit predictions graded by established metrics (e.g., AUROC, Spearman).

contributions.statement: Biomedical-specialized agent architectures do not provide consistent performance advantages over general-purpose ML agents on end-to-end biomedical ML tasks.
contributions.quote: biomedical specialization does not confer a consistent advantage

contributions.statement: Agent success on biomedical ML tasks is more strongly driven by diversity of ML strategies employed (e.g., feature engineering, model stacking) and overall architecture design than by domain-specific biomedical knowledge.
contributions.quote: agents which employed more diverse ML strategies more often tended to score highest, suggesting that architecture and scaffolding may be stronger determinants of performance

contributions.statement: Current LLM agents underperform human baselines on end-to-end biomedical ML workflows.
contributions.quote: all agents on average underperform human baselines

contributions.statement: CompBioBench is a 100-question benchmark spanning genomics, transcriptomics, epigenomics, single-cell analysis, human genetics, and machine learning, evaluating agentic systems on diverse problems requiring multi-step reasoning, tool use, external resource access, and autonomous strategy selection.
contributions.quote: We introduce CompBioBench, a benchmark of 100 diverse tasks for evaluating agentic systems in computational biology.

contributions.statement: Using synthetic/augmented data and metadata scrambling/scrubbing enables objective benchmark design for inherently noisy domains, producing unambiguous ground-truth answers while maintaining problem complexity and requiring multi-step reasoning, tool use, and autonomous strategy selection.
contributions.quote: we propose a new benchmark construction strategy based on synthetic/augmented data and metadata scrambling/scrubbing of real datasets to create challenging problems that have a single ground-truth answer and require multi-step reasoning, tool use, bespoke code, and interaction with real-world external resources.

contributions.statement: General-purpose agentic systems achieve strong overall performance on computational biology benchmarks (78–83% accuracy) but show significant performance degradation on harder problems (49–69%).
contributions.quote: We find strong end-to-end performance, with Codex CLI (GPT 5.4) reaching 83% accuracy, Gemini CLI (3.1 Pro) reaching 82%, Claude Code (Opus 4.6) reaching 81%, and Claude Code (Opus 4.7) reaching 78%. On the hardest questions, Claude Code (Opus 4.6) reaches 69%, Codex CLI (GPT 5.4) reaches 59%, and Gemini CLI (3.1 Pro) reaches 49%.

contributions.statement: SciAgents—a multi-agent framework that integrates large-scale ontological knowledge graphs with specialized LLM-based agents and data retrieval tools, assigning distinct roles through prompt engineering to automate scientific discovery.
contributions.quote: In this work, SciAgents, an approach that leverages three core concepts is presented: (1) large-scale ontological knowledge graphs to organize and interconnect diverse scientific concepts, (2) a suite of large language models (LLMs) and data retrieval tools, and (3) multi-agent systems with in-situ learning capabilities.

contributions.statement: A novel sub-graph sampling strategy that extracts contextually relevant subgraphs from large ontological knowledge graphs to contextualize and guide multi-agent hypothesis generation and scientific reasoning.
contributions.quote: We implemented a novel sampling strategy to extract relevant sub-graphs from this comprehensive knowledge graph, allowing us to identify and understand the key concepts and their interrelationships.

contributions.statement: Multi-agent systems with specialized roles can reveal previously hidden interdisciplinary relationships between scientific concepts through knowledge graph reasoning, achieving greater scale, precision, and exploratory power than human research methods.
contributions.quote: Applied to biologically inspired materials, SciAgents reveals hidden interdisciplinary relationships that were previously considered unrelated, achieving a scale, precision, and exploratory power that surpasses human research methods.

contributions.statement: A new biocomposite material with enhanced mechanical properties and improved sustainability achieved through energy-efficient production methods discovered via automated multi-agent scientific discovery.
contributions.quote: resulting in a new biocomposite with enhanced mechanical properties and improved sustainability through energy-efficient production is shown.

contributions.statement: Large language models can effectively structure scientific knowledge and generate interesting, testable scientific hypotheses, though with elevated error rates.
contributions.quote: generative AI seems to be able to effectively structure vast amounts of scientific knowledge and provide interesting and testable hypotheses

contributions.statement: A multi-agent scientific discovery framework combining AI-generated hypothesis generation, human curation, automated experimentation, and adversarial peer review to enable synergistic human-AI scientific research.
contributions.quote: The future scientific enterprise may include synergistic efforts with a swarm of "hypothesis machines", challenged by automated experimentation and adversarial peer reviews

contributions.statement: Peer review reformulated as a multi-turn, long-context dialogue with role-based interactions for authors, reviewers, and decision makers captures the iterative nature of academic peer review.
contributions.quote: we reformulate the peer-review process as a multi-turn, long-context dialogue, incorporating distinct roles for authors, reviewers, and decision makers

contributions.statement: ReviewMT — a dataset of 26,841 papers with 92,017 reviews from ICLR and Nature Communications designed for training and evaluating multi-turn dialogue systems in academic peer review.
contributions.quote: We construct a comprehensive dataset containing over 26,841 papers with 92,017 reviews collected from multiple sources, including the top-tier conference and prestigious journal. This dataset is meticulously designed to facilitate the applications of LLMs for multi-turn dialogues, effectively simulating the complete peer-review process.

contributions.statement: Role-specific evaluation metrics for assessing LLM performance in multi-turn peer-review dialogues, measuring response validity, text quality, score accuracy, and decision-making quality for each participant role.
contributions.quote: we propose a series of metrics to evaluate the performance of LLMs for each role under this reformulated peer-review setting, ensuring fair and comprehensive evaluations

contributions.statement: An interactive web-based system for multimodal peer review simulation integrating textual and visual information through multimodal LLMs and retrieval-augmented generation grounded in web-scale OpenReview data.
contributions.quote: We present the first open-source web demo system that integrates multimodal LLMs to deliver human-aligned review feedback, grounded in publicly available review datasets from OpenReview for the target venue.

contributions.statement: Action:Objective[#] — a structured format that distills peer review commentary into traceable, actionable revision items enabling authors to systematically apply feedback for manuscript improvement.
contributions.quote: We propose a structured and actionable to-do format that distills reviewer commentary into traceable revision items, enabling authors to systematically apply feedback for manuscript improvements before submission.

contributions.statement: Multimodal perception combining text and visual elements produces more comprehensive and balanced peer review assessments with reduced bias compared to text-only approaches.
contributions.quote: Empirical results demonstrate the critical role of multimodal perception in producing more comprehensive and balanced assessments, reducing bias, and enhancing feedback reliability and usefulness in academic workflows.

contributions.statement: Orion — a computer-using AI agent that integrates terminal code execution, graphical interface control, and adaptive multi-step reasoning to automate end-to-end biomedical image analysis and interpretation workflows without requiring bespoke software integrations.
contributions.quote: we present Orion, a computer-using AI agent for biomedical image analysis and interpretation that moves towards lab automation by automating this computational layer of laboratory work. Orion combines large language models with terminal execution, GUI control and adaptive multi-step reasoning in a shared computing environment. It can inspect visual data, operate standard scientific software, mine web resources and conduct end-to-end analysis and interpretation workflows without requiring bespoke software integrations.

contributions.statement: A hierarchical agent architecture with a terminal-based controller and dynamically spawned GUI subagents, combined with externalized memory, enables sustained autonomous execution of complex research workflows exceeding 300 sequential actions.
contributions.quote: Orion overcomes this via a hierarchical agent architecture that maximized reasoning efficiency while preventing context saturation. In this framework, a terminal-based controller manages global strategy and file organization, while specialized GUI subagents are dynamically spawned for intensive visual tasks or software operation. By "externalizing" intermediate results to the file system, Orion can sustain over 300 sequential actions

contributions.statement: Computer-using agents can learn to operate standard biomedical image analysis software from small numbers of public demonstrations, automatically adapting to new imaging conditions and adjusting analysis parameters through natural-language prompts.
contributions.quote: From a small number of public demos and tutorials, Orion learned to operate widely-used image analysis software, including CellProfiler and QuPath, enabling autonomous quantification of measured cellular and tissue features, characterization of subcellular morphology and segmentation of whole-slide pathology regions. Rather than relying on task-specific tuning, Orion generalized to new imaging settings through natural-language prompts and automatically adjusted analysis parameters to account for variations in cell size and imaging conditions.

contributions.statement: Computer-using agents can conduct autonomous discovery on large-scale biological imaging datasets, integrating quantitative measurements with knowledge from diverse sources to generate mechanistically-grounded research hypotheses that human scientists can validate and prioritize.
contributions.quote: Within 100 hours of autonomous exploration of a large-scale perturbation imaging dataset, Orion generated 52 research reports, of which human scientist review prioritized 22 plausible mechanistic hypotheses. Within 100 hours, Orion generated 52 detailed reports with mechanistically-grounded hypotheses by integrating imaging evidence with knowledge from diverse biological sources.

contributions.statement: A multi-agent architecture coordinating specialized large language model agents to decompose and replicate scientific publication methodologies through sequential tasks of document analysis, literature review, data acquisition, and code generation.
contributions.quote: By employing a multi-agent system composed of Large Language Models (LLMs), including both text and code generation agents built on OpenAI's platform, our study attempts to reproduce the methodology and findings of a high-impact publication

contributions.statement: Large language models demonstrate capability in scientific research automation for technical document summarization, literature-informed experimental design, simple statistical analysis tasks, and cross-concept inference without explicit instruction.
contributions.quote: Nevertheless, LLMs did show promise at replicating aspects of the scientific process. For example, the ability to analyze a technical document and extract concise summaries was performed expertly; further, the task of then surveying relevant literature to aid in experimental design, such as finding the marker genes necessary to guide scRNA-seq analyses, was also performed well. Lastly, the system was able to draw inferences and connect related concepts using its background knowledge, without being explicitly told to do so.

contributions.statement: Nondeterminism in code generation, data procurement bottlenecks, context length constraints, and training data bias represent critical obstacles limiting the effectiveness of large language model systems in replicating scientific research.
contributions.quote: However, we also identified significant challenges in the system, such as nondeterminism in code generation, difficulties in data procurement, and the limitations presented by context length and bias from the model's inherent training data.

contributions.statement: Idea Novelty Checker, a retrieval-augmented LLM framework using keyword and snippet-based retrieval, embedding-based filtering, and facet-based LLM re-ranking to assess whether scientific ideas are novel relative to prior literature
contributions.quote: We introduce Idea Novelty Checker, a retrieval-augmented LLM pipeline that automatically evaluates the novelty of scientific ideas. Idea Novelty Checker first collects a broad set of relevant papers using keyword and snippet-based retrieval, then refines this collection through embedding-based filtering followed by facet-based LLM re-ranking.

contributions.statement: Facet-based operationalization of novelty: an idea is novel if it differs in purpose, mechanism, evaluation, or application from papers in prior literature
contributions.quote: An idea is considered novel if it differs from all retrieved papers in at least one core facet for the topic at hand—namely, purpose (i.e., a distinct objective), mechanism (i.e., a distinct technical approach), or evaluation (i.e., a distinct validation method).

contributions.statement: Expert-annotated dataset of scientific ideas with novelty judgments, designed to support development and evaluation of automated novelty assessment systems
contributions.quote: We plan to release our code and expert-collected data to support work in automatic scientific discovery and provide literature-grounded novelty evaluations.

contributions.statement: Expert-annotated in-context examples improve LLM-based novelty evaluation accuracy by approximately 13% compared to existing approaches
contributions.quote: Our extensive experiments demonstrate that our novelty checker achieves approximately 13% higher agreement than existing approaches. Ablation studies further showcases the importance of the facet-based re-ranker in identifying the most relevant literature for novelty evaluation.

contributions.statement: Hybrid hypothesis generation combining inductive logic programming, large language models, and rigorous ontologies grounded in community knowledge and relational databases reduces hallucination while preserving language-driven flexibility.
contributions.quote: In this work, we introduce a hybrid approach combining LLMs with the precision of relational learning, inductive logic programming (ILP), and rigorous ontologies to generate hypotheses grounded in community knowledge, empirical data, and relational databases—mitigating hallucinations while preserving the flexibility of language-driven problem formulation.

contributions.statement: Automated scientific discovery pipeline integrating LLM-driven experimental design with robotic laboratory automation (liquid handling, cell culture systems, mass spectrometry-based metabolomics) for systematic generation and validation of biological hypotheses.
contributions.quote: We established an automated experimental and computational pipeline by integrating robotic liquid handling stations (a Hamilton Microlab Star and an Agilent Bravo), an automated laboratory cell (Eve) for cell culture and sampling, and mass spectrometry–based metabolomics (an Agilent 6560 IM-MS coupled to a 365-RapidFire) along with computational tools in R and Python. This pipeline is designed to automatically generate and experimentally validate hypotheses about cell biology.

contributions.statement: Description logic-based representation of scientific hypotheses with automated capture of hypotheses, experimental protocols, and empirical data in a graph database using controlled vocabularies and extended ontologies.
contributions.quote: All hypotheses, experiments, and data are captured in a graph database, using controlled vocabularies. The hypotheses and their implications are represented using description logics. Existing ontologies are extended, and a novel representation of scientific hypotheses is presented using description logics.

contributions.statement: Novel biological interactions in Saccharomyces cerevisiae identified through automated hypothesis validation, including glutamate-induced synergistic growth inhibition in spermine-treated cells and aminoadipate partial rescue of formic-acid stress.
contributions.quote: identifying novel interactions, including glutamate-induced synergistic growth inhibition in spermine-treated cells and aminoadipate's partial rescue of formic-acid stress.

contributions.statement: LiveIdeaBench evaluates LLMs' scientific idea generation under minimal context (single-keyword prompts) across five dimensions—originality, feasibility, fluency, flexibility, and clarity—using Guilford's divergent thinking framework.
contributions.quote: We introduce LiveIdeaBench, a comprehensive benchmark evaluating LLMs'scientific idea generation by assessing divergent thinking capabilities using single-keyword prompts. Drawing from Guilford'screativity theory, our benchmark employs a dynamic panel of state-of-the-art LLMs to assess generated ideas across five dimensions: originality, feasibility, fluency, flexibility, and clarity.

contributions.statement: Scientific idea generation capabilities in LLMs are decoupled from general intelligence; models with substantially different general intelligence scores can achieve comparable creative performance.
contributions.quote: we reveal that the scientiﬁc idea generation capabilities measured by our benchmark are poorly predicted by standard metrics of general intelligence scores. Our results demonstrate that models like QwQ-32B-preview achieve creative performance comparable to models such as claude-3.7-sonnet: thinking, despite signiﬁcant gaps in their general intelligence scores.

contributions.statement: Improving scientific idea generation in LLMs requires different training strategies than those optimizing for general problem-solving abilities, enabling development of specialized AI tools for different scientific discovery stages.
contributions.quote: These findings highlight the need for specialized evaluation benchmarks for scientific idea generation and suggest that enhancing these idea generation capabilities in LLMs may require different training strategies than those used for improving general problem-solving abilities. Such strategies could potentially enable a wider range of AI tools tailored for different stages of the scientiﬁc process.

contributions.statement: Comprehensive taxonomy of limitation types in scientific research papers, with focus on AI
contributions.quote: We first present a comprehensive taxonomy of limitation types in scientific research, with a focus on AI

contributions.statement: LimitGen — a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating LLMs' ability to identify and address limitations in research papers
contributions.quote: we present LimitGen , the first comprehensive benchmark for evaluating LLMs'capability to support early-stage feedback and complement human peer review

contributions.statement: LimitGen-Syn — synthetic dataset created through controlled perturbations of papers to systematically introduce specific types of research limitations
contributions.quote: LimitGen -Syn, a synthetic dataset carefully created through controlled perturbations of high-quality papers

contributions.statement: LimitGen-Human — dataset of real limitations written by human reviewers in scientific peer review
contributions.quote: LimitGen -Human, a collection of real human-written limitations

contributions.statement: RAG-augmented approach for limitation identification that grounds analysis in retrieved prior literature
contributions.quote: we augment them with literature retrieval, which is essential for grounding identifying limitations in prior scientific findings... Our approach enhances the capabilities of LLM systems to generate limitations in research papers, enabling them to provide more concrete and constructive feedback

contributions.statement: AstroInsight — an end-to-end framework for automated scientific idea generation in data-driven astronomy — integrates fact extraction and hypothesis mining, entity linking-based iterative refinement, Mixture of Agents optimization, and human-AI collaborative review.
contributions.quote: we implement a novel framework, AstroInsight, that integrates conception, iterative refinement, expert validation, and knowledge integration for idea generation

contributions.statement: Entity linking-based iterative optimization combining entity refinements with re-ranking improves the accuracy and coherence of scientific hypotheses.
contributions.quote: We design an iterative optimization framework that combines entity linking-based refinements with re-ranking, improving the accuracy and coherence of hypotheses.

contributions.statement: Mixture of Agents architecture where multiple LLMs independently refine scientific drafts and merge outputs enables deeper iterations and facilitates disruptive discovery.
contributions.quote: We use a MoA architecture to integrate AI reasoning with cross-domain knowledge, enabling deeper draft iterations and facilitating disruptive scientific discovery.

contributions.statement: Human-AI collaborative quality control mechanism integrating expert evaluation with LLM-based assessment assures scientific validity while maintaining efficiency.
contributions.quote: We establish a quality control mechanism that integrates expert evaluation with LLMs to assure scientific validity while maintaining high efficiency.

contributions.statement: LLM-generated research ideas in data-driven astronomy achieve novelty scores of 3+/6 and match or exceed human-generated ideas in originality and feasibility.
contributions.quote: with the generated drafts achieving a novelty score of 3+/6 in both human- and model-based evaluations. Additionally, its generated ideas match or even exceed human-generated ones in originality and feasibility across multiple data-driven astronomical topics.

contributions.statement: TreeReview—a hierarchical question-answer framework for scientific peer review that recursively decomposes review tasks into sub-questions, answers them using focused paper chunks, and aggregates answers bottom-up with dynamic question expansion for deeper analysis
contributions.quote: we propose TreeReview, a novel framework that models paper review as a hierarchical and bidirectional question-answering process. TreeReview first constructs a tree of review questions by recursively decomposing high-level questions into fine-grained sub-questions and then resolves the question tree by iteratively aggregating answers from leaf to root to get the final review. Crucially, we incorporate a dynamic question expansion mechanism to enable deeper probing by generating follow-up questions when needed.

contributions.statement: An evaluation benchmark for scientific peer review assessment supporting both full review generation and actionable feedback comment generation, derived from ICLR and NeurIPS papers
contributions.quote: We construct a benchmark derived from ICLR and NeurIPS venues to evaluate our method on full review generation and actionable feedback comments generation tasks.

contributions.statement: Hierarchical question decomposition with dynamic expansion generates comprehensive, expert-aligned scientific reviews with 80% token reduction compared to multi-agent review approaches
contributions.quote: Experimental results of both LLM-based and human evaluation show that TreeReview outperforms strong baselines in providing comprehensive, in-depth, and expert-aligned review feedback, while reducing LLM token usage by up to 80% compared to computationally intensive approaches.

contributions.statement: GeneBench is a benchmark of 103 problems across 10 domains in genomics and quantitative biology that evaluates AI agent performance on realistic multi-stage scientific data analysis workflows spanning data quality control, exploratory analysis, model selection, diagnostics, and iterative refinement with verifiable answers.
contributions.quote: We introduce GeneBench, a benchmark for AI agents on realistic multi-stage scientific data analysis in genetics and quantitative biology. ... GeneBench addresses this gap with 103 evaluations targeting quantities of direct practical relevance across 10 domains, with a genomics-centered core and adjacent coverage in other 'omics and quantitative biology settings. Each problem comprises an encapsulated multi-step analysis with staged data, prompts that define a quantity of interest while otherwise providing minimal guidance, and verifiable answers.

contributions.statement: AI models frequently identify diagnostic signals in scientific data analysis but fail to propagate those observations into corresponding analytical decisions, resulting in incorrect method selection or persistence with initially plausible but ultimately incorrect analysis paths.
contributions.quote: Models often complete substantial portions of the workflow but exhibit a consistent gap between noticing and acting: they identify local diagnostic signals but fail to propagate the implication to the corresponding analysis decision, and as a result select wrong estimators or persist on initially plausible but incorrect analysis paths.

contributions.statement: A GPT-4-based pipeline that generates structured scientific feedback on research papers, providing assessments of significance, novelty, potential acceptance/rejection reasoning, and improvement suggestions.
contributions.quote: Specifically, we developed a Generative Pretrained Transformer 4 (GPT-4)–based scientific feedback generation pipeline that takes the raw PDF of a paper and produces structured feedback... The system is designed to generate constructive feedback across various key aspects, mirroring the review structure of leading interdisciplinary journals and conferences, including significance and novelty, potential reasons for acceptance, potential reasons for rejection, and suggestions for improvement.

contributions.statement: LLM-generated feedback on research papers overlaps with individual human peer reviews at rates comparable to the overlap between two human reviewers, across multiple scientific domains and large-scale datasets.
contributions.quote: The overlap in the points raised by GPT-4 and by human reviewers (average overlap of 30.85% for Nature journals and 39.23% for ICLR) is comparable with the overlap between two human reviewers (average overlap of 28.58% for Nature journals and 35.25% for ICLR).

contributions.statement: Researchers perceive LLM-generated feedback on their own papers as helpful and beneficial, with a majority finding it useful and most rating it competitive with or superior to human peer review feedback.
contributions.quote: In our prospective user study, more than half (57.4%) of the users found GPT-4–generated feedback helpful/very helpful, and 82.4% found it more beneficial than feedback from at least some human reviewers.

contributions.statement: A typed, dynamic measurable architecture for certifying and releasing autonomous research claims that decomposes into primitive public state, declared authority algebras, authority profiles, constructive typed transcripts, versioned release units, certified tokens, and fail-closed persistent failure shadows.
contributions.quote: a typed, dynamic measurable architecture with primitive public states, declared authority algebras, authority profiles, constructive typed transcripts, versioned release units, active certified tokens, shadow-eligible footprint-scoped persistent failure shadows, and explicit lifecycle transitions

contributions.statement: A non-circular formulation of no-meta governance where policies are defined by measurability with respect to a declared authority sigma-field, with proof that the typed transcript is a lossless constructive generator of that sigma-field.
contributions.quote: a non-circular formulation of no-meta governance in which the policy class is defined by measurability with respect to the declared authority sigma-field, followed by a theorem that the typed transcript is a lossless constructive generator of that sigma-field and a representative-free minimality theorem for binary realized no-meta decisions

contributions.statement: An authority-import discipline with constitutional type governance, public activation and migration rules, grounded proof-carrying imports, and coherence constraints that prevent arbitrary executions from creating spurious policy authority.
contributions.quote: a strengthened authority-import discipline with constitutional type governance, public activation and migration rules, grounded proof-carrying imports, authority-eligible execution constraints, and a current-validity coherence rule that prevents arbitrary failing executions from creating spurious policy authority

contributions.statement: A two-layer certifiability frontier separating structural derivation from operational certification, with exclusion theorems stated in terms of public witnesses, completion, and transport.
contributions.quote: a two-layer certifiability frontier separating structural derivation of public separation from verifier-valid public failure certificates and repair witnesses, together with structural and operational exclusion theorems stated directly in terms of public witnesses, completion, and transport

contributions.statement: A public inferential shell with realized finite revealed graphs, declared event budgets, predictable e-processes, and seal nodes that enables program-level validity certification for sealed inferential admission.
contributions.quote: a public inferential shell with realized finite revealed graphs, declared event budgets, predictable e-factor programs, and seal nodes, together with a program-level validity theorem for sealed inferential admission

contributions.statement: BioMedAgent — a self-evolving multi-agent LLM framework that interprets natural-language prompts to autonomously plan, code, and execute complex biomedical data analyses by integrating diverse bioinformatics tools into executable workflows.
contributions.quote: Here we introduce BioMedAgent, a self-evolving LLM multi-agent framework, which learns to use diverse bioinformatics tools and chain them into executable workflows through interactive exploration and memory retrieval algorithms.

contributions.statement: A memory retrieval algorithm that systematically records and reuses successful tool selections, workflows, and executed codes to enable dynamic self-evolution and adaptation as the system encounters similar tasks.
contributions.quote: To enable self-evolving capabilities, BioMedAgent incorporates the memory retrieval (MR) algorithm, which uses a retrieval-augmented mechanism to facilitate the reuse of successful experiences. It systematically records effective tool selections, workflows and executed codes, reusing and updating these memories with new insights as the system encounters similar tasks.
